findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — partitioned indexes are rare in OutSystems estates and the trigger needs malformed JSON
findings.empiricalCheck: Unit-level: feed tryProjectDataSpace a PARTITION_SCHEME with unparseable PartitionColumnsJson and render the CREATE INDEX; observe ON [scheme] with no column list.

findings.topic: Deployed-storage (on-disk) precedence for ordinary scalars

v1.behavior: TypeMappingPolicy.Resolve tries the on-disk (deployed) SQL type FIRST for every non-identifier attribute; deployed type wins over both external dbType and the logical mapping (only 'date' logical type refuses a non-date on-disk type).

v2.behavior: resolveAttributeType now adopts deployed #ColumnReality storage for ordinary scalars when it is a SAME-category refinement (toPrimitiveType deployed = logical primitive), and columnStorageDivergences emits a per-column Warning naming every logical-vs-deployed storage disagreement, wired into LiveModelRead.

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c1 ('ordinary logical types are never widened by deployed reality; deployed type drift undiagnosed') is STALE — fixed on main 2026-07-16 (WP-4b, DECISIONS 2026-07-16; register C1 marked Landed). On the live lane a deployed same-category widening (e.g. text(50) deployed NVARCHAR(100)) now emits the deployed width in both pipelines, with V2 additionally warning per column.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — physically widened columns are common in aged estates; pre-fix V2 would have emitted narrower columns than deployed and truncated on load
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane against localhost:11433: seed OSSYS metadata declaring text(50) but deploy the physical column as NVARCHAR(100); run V2 publish and V1 full-export; both Modules/*.sql must show NVARCHAR(100), and V2's diagnostics must contain adapter.ossys.columnReality.storageDivergence for that column.

findings.topic: Cross-category deployed retype (e.g. logical boolean deployed TINYINT)

v1.behavior: On-disk rule table covers every SQL base type, so a cross-category deployed retype (boolean->tinyint, text->int) emits the DEPLOYED type with no diagnostic; only logical 'date' is guarded (ShouldUseOnDisk).

v2.behavior: A cross-category deployed type is treated as a conflict, not a refinement: the LOGICAL storage is emitted and the divergence is named per-column (emits=logical in the Warning metadata).

findings.difference: For a cross-category physically-retyped column V1 reproduces deployed reality; V2 emits the model type and warns. After cutover the column's type changes back to the logical mapping under V2 (deploy will attempt a type change; data may fail conversion), whereas V1 preserves the deployed type.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — requires a DBA-retyped column whose SQL type crosses V2's 9-way category boundary; rare in native estates, real at the External-Entity/DBA edge
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: deploy a logical Boolean attribute as TINYINT in warm SQL (localhost:11433); V1 full-export emits TINYINT, V2 publish emits BIT plus a storageDivergence Warning with emits=logical.

findings.topic: 2000-char NVARCHAR(MAX)/VARBINARY(MAX) promotion applied to evidence channels

v1.behavior: The 2000 maxLengthThreshold applies on ALL THREE channels: logical, on-disk, and external (onDisk nvarchar/varbinary and external nvarchar/varbinary rules carry maxLengthThreshold 2000), so a deployed NVARCHAR(3000) or an external dbType NVARCHAR(3000) is promoted to NVARCHAR(MAX).

v2.behavior: The >=2000 -> Max promotion exists ONLY on the logical text channel (OssysTypeMapping.textLength); deployed and external evidence parse through SqlStorageType.ofSqlType which keeps any positive bounded length faithfully (NVARCHAR(3000) stays Bounded 3000).

findings.difference: Deployed/external widths in [2000, 4000) for nvarchar (and >=2000 for varbinary) emit as MAX in V1 but as their faithful bounded width in V2 — a storage-class change (in-row vs LOB, index-key eligibility) between the two outputs. V2 is more faithful to deployed reality; V1 matches the platform's own MAX convention.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — needs a deployed or external column with declared width in the 2000-3999 range (nvarchar) or >=2000 (varbinary)
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: deploy a text attribute as NVARCHAR(3000); V1 Modules/*.sql shows NVARCHAR(MAX), V2 shows NVARCHAR(3000).

findings.topic: external dbType vs deployed-storage precedence order

v1.behavior: On-disk (deployed) beats external dbType: TryResolveFromOnDisk returns before the ExternalDatabaseType branch is reached.

v2.behavior: external dbType (the authored override) beats deployedStorage: the match arms consult externalOverride first; deployed is consulted only when no external type is present.

findings.difference: Precedence is inverted. For an external entity whose authored External_Column_Type says VARCHAR(50) but whose deployed column is VARCHAR(100): V1 emits VARCHAR(100) (deployed), V2 emits VARCHAR(50) (authored). V2's ordering treats the authored override as intent; V1 treats deployment as truth.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only external entities where authored dbType and deployed storage disagree
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: seed an external attribute with External_Column_Type='varchar(50)' over a deployed VARCHAR(100) column; diff the emitted column between V1 and V2.

findings.topic: Model-JSON lane: deployed evidence carriage

v1.behavior: The model JSON carries the full onDisk sub-object (sqlType, maxLength, precision, scale, collation, isIdentity, defaultConstraint, checkConstraints) produced by the shared rowsets SQL, and Osm.Json deserializes it into AttributeOnDiskMetadata which the emitter consumes.

v2.behavior: OssysJsonReader passes None for deployedStorage ('The JSON projection does not carry parsed deployed-storage evidence — its onDisk sub-object is not lifted') and sets Collation = None, Identity = None, Computed = None on the JSON path; deployed evidence exists only on the live rowset path.

findings.difference: When both pipelines are fed the same model JSON (the fixture lane), V1 honors deployed types/collation/identity/defaults embedded in the JSON while V2 ignores that sub-object entirely — so V2's WP-4b deployed precedence, collation carriage, and computed/default-name lifts are LIVE-LANE ONLY. Any fixture-lane byte comparison will diverge on every column where onDisk differs from logical.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — irrelevant for a live-connection cutover, decisive for teams exporting from a saved model.json snapshot
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: feed the same model.json (with onDisk sqlType nvarchar(100) over a text(50) attribute) to V1 build-ssdt and V2 projection publish; V1 emits NVARCHAR(100), V2 emits NVARCHAR(50) with no diagnostic.

findings.topic: email/phone storage type (WP-4)

v1.behavior: Logical rule maps email -> VARCHAR(250) and phone/phonenumber -> VARCHAR(20) (VarCharText strategy, ANSI); on live runs the rule rarely fired because deployed on-disk NVARCHAR won precedence.

v2.behavior: Since WP-4 (DECISIONS 2026-07-16): email -> NVARCHAR(boundedOr 250), phone -> NVARCHAR(boundedOr 20) — platform-native storage; declared length wins; no MAX promotion on these two.

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c3 ('two ANSI islands') is STALE — fixed on main 2026-07-16. V2's logical mapping now matches platform reality (ossys_User.EMAIL is nvarchar(250)) so live-lane output agrees with V1's on-disk-won output; only an estate whose email column has NO deployed presence (fresh logical-only attribute) now differs from V1's raw logical rule (V1 VARCHAR vs V2 NVARCHAR — V2 deliberate, register C3 Landed). Residual edge: neither side MAX-promotes email/phone, so an authored length >= 4001 yields V1 VARCHAR(5000) (valid) vs V2 NVARCHAR(5000) (invalid, DacFx build error).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every estate has User.Email/Phone columns; post-fix the live outputs converge
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: extract an estate with ossys_User; both V1 and V2 emit [EMAIL] NVARCHAR (250); fixture lane with a logical-only email attribute and no onDisk shows V1 VARCHAR(250) vs V2 NVARCHAR(250).

findings.topic: Unknown/unmapped OutSystems type string

v1.behavior: Unknown logical types fall to the default rule (UnicodeText, threshold 2000) and silently emit NVARCHAR(len) or NVARCHAR(MAX).

v2.behavior: OssysTypeMapping.tryParse returns None and the adapter refuses the whole build with named error adapter.osm.unmappedDataType.

findings.difference: V1 guesses (silent NVARCHAR), V2 refuses loudly and blocks the export until the type is mapped. Operator impact at cutover: an estate carrying an exotic ossys type string exports under V1 and hard-fails under V2.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — depends on the estate carrying type strings outside the mapped set (old platform versions, custom extensions)
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: add an attribute with Type='SomethingExotic' to the rowset seed; V1 emits NVARCHAR(MAX); V2 publish fails with adapter.osm.unmappedDataType naming the type.

findings.topic: Mandatory column whose data contains NULLs (nullability authority)

v1.behavior: Under default EvidenceGated mode (config default-tightening.json mode=EvidenceGated, nullBudget 0), the S5 mandatory signal FAILS when profiling shows nulls beyond budget, so the column emits NULLABLE (silent loosening, rationale data_has_nulls; no remediation flag outside Aggressive). Cautious mode instead forces NOT NULL + requiresRemediation; Aggressive forces NOT NULL + remediation pre-scripts.

v2.behavior: Emits NOT NULL from Is_Mandatory verbatim (Column.IsNullable = not IsMandatory; emission Nullable = keepNullable || (IsNullable && not enforceNotNull)); legacy NULLs surface as data-fidelity violations (estate DataNotNull finding lane) and fail the load by design; the only lawful loosening is the operator's explicit keepNullable override.

findings.difference: THE central nullability divergence at cutover: same estate with dirty data -> V1 (default mode) publishes a NULLABLE column and the load succeeds carrying the legacy NULLs; V2 publishes NOT NULL and the load flags/refuses until the operator remediates data or merges a keepNullable overlay entry. V1 = automatic evidence-driven schema adjustment; V2 = operator-in-the-loop with named findings.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — mandatory-but-physically-nullable with legacy NULLs is endemic in real OutSystems estates (isMandatory is logical-only)
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane against localhost:11433: mandatory Text attribute over a nullable deployed column with one NULL row; V1 full-export Modules/*.sql shows NULL for that column; V2 publish shows NOT NULL and fidelity output itemizes the violating rows.

findings.topic: Non-mandatory column that is physically NOT NULL

v1.behavior: S2_DB_NOT_NULL signal (profile.IsNullablePhysical=false) tightens the emitted column to NOT NULL in ALL three modes — deployed constraint preserved.

v2.behavior: Emits NULL (Is_Mandatory=false -> IsNullable=true); the deployed NOT NULL is reported only inside ONE aggregated Info diagnostic (adapter.ossys.columnReality.nullabilityDivergence with per-direction counts and a 5-column sample), and the EnforceNotNull machinery that could restore it is config-unreachable (interventions bind RelaxationOnly).

findings.difference: A DBA/hand-tightened NOT NULL disappears from the published schema under V2 (data still loads, but the integrity constraint is dropped with only an aggregate Info line); V1 preserves it. Severity is real for estates that hardened columns outside Service Studio.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — requires physically-NOT-NULL columns not marked mandatory in the model (DBA hardening, extension tables)
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: non-mandatory attribute deployed NOT NULL; V1 emits NOT NULL, V2 emits NULL and its diagnostics carry logicalNullableDeployedNotNull >= 1.

findings.topic: Config-driven nullability coercion / relaxation reachability

v1.behavior: Three config-selectable modes (Cautious/EvidenceGated/Aggressive) plus nullBudget, allowCautiousNullabilityRelaxation, and per-module nullabilityOverrides that relax specific columns; the policy always runs in full-export step 3.

v2.behavior: TighteningBinding.fromConfig DROPS any nullability entry carrying a nullBudget (the coercion direction stays neutralized per DECISIONS 2026-06-22), but since the 2026-07-15 estate A6 amendment a budget-LESS entry binds as a RelaxationOnly intervention whose keepNullable overrides act (DecisionOverlay.KeepNullable, minted only from OperatorOverride); NullabilityTighteningConfig.create (EvidenceDriven) has zero production callers — tests only (V1NullabilityParityTests maps V1's modes and documents Aggressive as having no V2 equivalent).

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c6 verified with one refinement: the 2026-06-22 total config no-op was AMENDED 2026-07-15 — keepNullable relaxation overrides are config-reachable again (V1-parity for the relax direction); NULL->NOT NULL coercion remains config-unreachable, and V1's Aggressive/Cautious forced-tightening and FK/unique-evidence-driven tightening of non-mandatory columns have no V2 counterpart at all.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — an operator migrating a V1 tightening config must know the mode/nullBudget knobs are silently dropped by V2, not honored
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: config with tightening intervention kind=nullability + nullBudget 0.05 -> V2 publish output identical to no-config run; same entry without nullBudget but with a keepNullable override -> that one column emits NULL.

findings.topic: IDENTITY flag source (logical Is_AutoNumber vs deployed is_identity)

v1.behavior: isIdentity = onDisk.IsIdentity ?? attribute.IsAutoNumber — the DEPLOYED is_identity bit wins whenever the extraction captured it; the model flag is only the fallback.

v2.behavior: IsIdentity = row.IsAutoNumber (logical), and F9 columnRealityDivergences emits a per-column Warning when logical identity disagrees with deployed is_identity ('The engine carries the LOGICAL value').

findings.difference: Authority inverted: model-autonumber-but-deployed-non-identity emits IDENTITY under V2 (with warning) and plain column under V1; deployed-identity-but-not-autonumber emits IDENTITY under V1 and plain under V2. Changes insert behavior and bulk-load IDENTITY_INSERT requirements for the divergent columns.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — identity divergence is rare in native estates, plausible on external/hand-migrated tables
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: deploy an autonumber attribute over a non-identity column; V1 emits no IDENTITY clause, V2 emits IDENTITY (1, 1) plus adapter.ossys.columnReality.identityDivergence.

findings.topic: Deployed (hand-added) DEFAULT constraints

v1.behavior: ResolveDefaultExpression prefers deployed evidence: onDisk.DefaultDefinition (from sys.default_constraints via extraction) first, then profiling-discovered defaults (ColumnProfile.DefaultDefinition via BuildProfileDefaults), then the authored attribute DefaultValue; the deployed constraint NAME is emitted verbatim including SQL Server auto-names (no DF__ filter).

v2.behavior: Authored channel ONLY: Default_Value lifts (with empty/whitespace suppressed as no-op) on both live and JSON paths; the reflected #ColumnReality.DefaultDefinition is deliberately NOT lifted (named trigger on record, matrix row 53); the deployed DefaultConstraintName is lifted only when it is not a DF__ auto-name.

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c9 confirmed and still current: a DBA-added DEFAULT (e.g. getutcdate()) is reproduced by V1 and absent from V2's output — post-cutover inserts relying on it get NULL/failure under V2. Secondary deltas: V1 pins source-instance DF__ auto-names into DDL (couples output to the source instance), V2 filters them and lets SQL Server re-auto-name; V1's authored-BIT default normalization (True->1) is mirrored by V2's BooleanLit rendering.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — hand-added defaults are common in mature on-prem estates; authored defaults are at parity
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: add DEFAULT (getutcdate()) to a deployed column with no authored default; V1 Modules/*.sql carries the DEFAULT (with the DF__ name), V2's file has no DEFAULT for that column.

findings.topic: Empty-string authored default on the JSON lane

v1.behavior: AttributeModel.Create trims empty/whitespace defaults to null and ResolveDefaultExpression drops them — no DEFAULT clause on any path.

v2.behavior: Rowset path suppresses empty/whitespace (no DEFAULT); but the JSON path renders default:'' on a NON-text type as SqlLiteral.NullLit -> an emitted DEFAULT NULL clause (kept deliberately at its pre-WP-3 rendering; on Text it now emits DEFAULT N'').

findings.difference: Refines claim dec-defaults-suppression: the no-op-default suppression holds on the live/rowset path, but the JSON lane emits a literal DEFAULT NULL constraint for a non-text empty authored default where V1 emits nothing — a schema-compare-visible constraint object with no behavioral effect on a nullable column.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only JSON-lane exports of snapshots carrying default:'' on non-text attributes
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: model.json attribute {type: Integer, default: ""}; V2 emits DEFAULT NULL on the column, V1 emits no default clause.

findings.topic: Per-column COLLATE emission rules

v1.behavior: Consumes the shared rowsets contract (NULLIF against DATABASEPROPERTYEX suppresses source-DB-default collations at extraction) but ADDITIONALLY suppresses any column whose surviving collation equals Latin1_General_CI_AI at emission (hard-coded case-insensitive filter).

v2.behavior: Same byte-equal extraction contract (diff-verified identical file); every carried (non-source-default) collation re-emits as COLLATE <name> unquoted with no further filtering (F1).

findings.difference: On a database whose default is NOT Latin1_General_CI_AI but which has CI_AI-collated columns (Azure/platform-pinned columns), V1 silently drops the COLLATE clause (the column drifts to the target default at deploy) while V2 preserves it. Both suppress source-default collations identically at extraction (pkt-c8 extraction half confirmed, and it is shared V1 behavior too since the SQL is byte-equal).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — fires only when column collation is CI_AI and the DB default is something else
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: on a CP1_CI_AS-default warm DB, deploy a column COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AI; V1 emits no COLLATE, V2 emits COLLATE Latin1_General_CI_AI.

findings.topic: .sqlproj collation pins

v1.behavior: Pins DSP Sql160, ModelCollation '1033,CI' AND DefaultCollation 'SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS' in the generated .sqlproj.

v2.behavior: Pins DSP Sql160 and ModelCollation '1033, CI' only — no DefaultCollation element (default-collation columns inherit the target DB default at deploy).

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c8's sqlproj half confirmed, plus a V1-side delta the claim missed: V1 additionally pins the DacFx model's DefaultCollation to CP1_CI_AS, so dacpac-based schema compares from V1's project can generate collation-change diffs against targets with a different default; V2's project stays collation-neutral. (Cosmetic: '1033,CI' vs '1033, CI' whitespace.)
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — matters only for dacpac/schema-compare deployment flows onto non-CP1_CI_AS targets
findings.empiricalCheck: Static: inspect both generated .sqlproj files from a fixture-lane run; optionally dacpac-build both and compare model default collations.

findings.topic: V2 round-trip/diff comparator blindness (collation + within-category storage)

v1.behavior: No equivalent round-trip canary exists; V1 verifies via ScriptDom re-parse and DMM lens only, so nothing comparable to be blind.

v2.behavior: PhysicalColumn carries Type : PrimitiveType (9-way) with no Collation and no SqlStorageType field, and CatalogDiff's AttributeFacet set is {DataType(=PrimitiveType), Nullability, PrimaryKey, Length, Precision, Scale, Identity, DefaultValue, Computed} — no collation facet, no concrete-storage facet.

findings.difference: Confirms register C8's 'collation drift is invisible to the round-trip proof' and extends it: any within-category concrete-type drift (INT<->BIGINT, DATETIME<->DATETIME2, VARCHAR<->NVARCHAR at equal length) is also invisible to V2's canary/diff/migration facets because comparison happens at the 9-way PrimitiveType grain. Not a V1 regression (V1 has no canary), but a limit an operator must know before trusting the round-trip proof.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the canary is V2's cutover evidence; its blind spots bound what N=10-green actually proves
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: after a V2 publish+deploy, manually ALTER a column INT->BIGINT (or change its collation) on the target and re-run the canary/check; confirm no diff facet is reported.

findings.topic: SQL version pin uniformity (pkt-b5)

v1.behavior: MIXED: Sql150ScriptGenerator for emission (SmoContext), TSql150Parser for expression/predicate parse and post-emission validation, but DSP Sql160DatabaseSchemaProvider in the .sqlproj.

v2.behavior: Uniform Sql160: Sql160ScriptGenerator (pinned options), thread-local TSql160Parser, Sql160 DSP, dacpac model.

findings.difference: Claim pkt-b5 confirmed for V2 and refined for V1: V1 is internally inconsistent (150 generator/parser vs 160 project DSP). Practical effect is limited to generator whitespace/keyword nuances and parse acceptance of 2022-only syntax in check/computed expressions (V1's TSql150 parse failure silently DROPS a default/check expression, CreateTableStatementBuilder.cs:391-410, while V2's TSql160 fallback wraps raw text).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only if deployed check/computed/default expressions use post-2019 syntax
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: seed a CHECK constraint using SQL2022-only syntax; V1 drops it from the emitted table, V2 carries it.

findings.topic: datetime DDL fallback + temporal literal CAST forms (pkt-c4 / WP-17d)

v1.behavior: DDL: logical datetime -> legacy DATETIME (config); data plane: every temporal literal wrapped in explicit CAST — datetime2(7)/date/time(7)/datetimeoffset(7) (with the audit's nuance that live SQL reads land in the DateTime/TimeSpan branches: date columns emit CAST(... AS datetime2(7)) and time columns emit a bare quoted TimeSpan string).

v2.behavior: Since WP-17(d) all three fallback sites carry legacy DATETIME (sqlDataTypeOption, ofPrimitiveType, baseName) so goldens now show DATETIME; temporal literals render V1's explicit CAST forms via DateTimeLit/DateLit/TimeLit/DateTimeOffsetLit; storage-evidence lane unchanged (datetime2 source still emits DATETIME2(7)).

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c4 confirmed and its two known gaps (fallback-lane DATETIME2 upgrade; bare un-CAST literals) are FIXED on main 2026-07-16 (WP-17(d), audit S3 marked LANDED, goldens repinned). Residual byte nuance vs V1's LIVE path: V2 emits CAST(... AS date)/CAST(... AS time(7)) for Date/Time categories while V1's SqlClient-fed live path emits CAST(... AS datetime2(7)) for date columns and a bare quoted string for time columns — value-identical after implicit conversion.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every estate has datetime attributes; the loaded values are identical, the literal text differs from V1's live bytes
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: static entity with Date and Time attributes; diff the seed scripts — V1 bootstrap shows CAST(... AS datetime2(7)) / bare '...' where V2 StaticSeeds.sql shows CAST(... AS date) / CAST(... AS time(7)); load both and compare stored values (equal).

findings.topic: Data-plane scalar carriage for float/real/datetimeoffset/xml/control-chars/empty-string (audit S1-S6)

v1.behavior: Native CLR object carriage end-to-end; double at G17, float at G9, DateTimeOffset CAST with offset, CR/LF/TAB spliced to CHAR() concatenation, N'' preserved — never collapses (audit section 9 confirms for both static-seed and live phased-MERGE paths).

v2.behavior: All six audit hazards verified LANDED in code: G17/G9 shape-driven float parse (Bulk.fs:59-71), offset-bearing DateTimeOffsetFormat + DateTimeOffsetLit CAST (RawValueCodec.fs:49-59,169-189; SqlLiteral.fs:67-76,157), fallback DATETIME (S3), CHAR() splice via shared textLiteralSegments (SqlLiteral.fs:113-128), xml/image/text/ntext cast-compare MERGE guard (ScriptDomBuild.fs:912-934), option-grain cells with '' surviving and loud refusal on non-empty-capable types (SqlLiteral.fs:140-150).

findings.difference: The audit's headline regressions (S1 float/real precision+overflow, S2 datetimeoffset zone-drop+throw, S4 raw control bytes, S5 xml MERGE compile error, S6 ''->NULL erasure) are all fixed on main 2026-07-16 with Docker gallery witnesses (ScalarCarriageRoundTripTests) — V2's data plane is now at or above V1 fidelity on every scalar while keeping CDC-silence; the 28->9 collapse remains structural but each collapsing type now has a faithful raw shape or a named refusal.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — the four contested types only enter via DBA columns/External Entities, exactly the cutover boundary
findings.empiricalCheck: Docker lane: run scripts/test.sh docker focus ScalarCarriageRoundTrip against localhost:11433 — float Double.MaxValue, real G9, datetimeoffset -03:00, xml content probes, control-char text all round-trip with the CDC-armed MERGE compiling twice.

findings.topic: Entity-reference (FK) column type derivation

v1.behavior: ResolveAttributeDataType re-resolves the REFERENCED entity's preferred identifier's type for every reference attribute — a reference to a text-keyed or integer-keyed (external) entity emits NVARCHAR/INT etc., not blanket BIGINT.

v2.behavior: 'entityreference' and bt<espace>*<entity> map to BIGINT by convention; bt* attributes ALWAYS adopt external/deployed storage when present (re-projecting the primitive), so a live bt* reference over textual storage emits the deployed type; 'entityreference'-typed attributes are NOT in the bt* channel — a cross-category deployed type keeps BIGINT (warning only); JSON path has no deployed channel so all references emit BIGINT there.

findings.difference: Different derivation strategies converge for native bigint-keyed estates but can diverge for non-bigint-keyed targets: V1 follows the target PK's resolved type on every lane; V2 follows deployed storage for bt* on the live lane only, and hard-BIGINTs 'entityreference'-typed attributes with cross-category deployed storage and ALL references on the JSON lane — a text-keyed external reference would emit a BIGINT column there (hydration-breaking if data flowed).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only estates with external entities keyed by non-bigint (text/guid/int) PKs; native OutSystems references are always bigint-keyed
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: seed a bt*-typed reference attribute deployed as NVARCHAR(50) to a text-keyed external entity; V2 must emit NVARCHAR(50) (deployed adoption); repeat via model-JSON fixture — V2 emits BIGINT there while V1 emits the target PK type.

findings.topic: identifier/autonumber/longinteger -> BIGINT forced over all evidence (pkt-c2)

v1.behavior: ShouldPreferRuntimeMapping short-circuits on-disk AND external evidence for IsIdentifier/IsAutoNumber attributes and for the identifier/autonumber/longinteger type strings — a deployed INT id still emits BIGINT, silently.

v2.behavior: isForcedRuntimeMapping ('identifier'/'autonumber'/'longinteger') ignores external and deployed evidence identically (register C2: the INT-vs-BIGINT call is an estate-verification decision), but the divergence is now NAMED per column by columnStorageDivergences (emits=logical).

findings.difference: PARITY on the emitted bytes (both force BIGINT even over a deployed 32-bit INT id — the packet's storage/index-width concern applies equally to both pipelines); V2 additionally surfaces a per-column warning V1 lacks. One V1-only subtlety: an IsIdentifier attribute whose declared type string is 'Integer' resolves via the integer rule to INT in both pipelines (the forcing keys on the type string, so behavior matches). Golden-gallery note: the catalog-direct golden PK shows INT IDENTITY because it rides the evidence-less PrimitiveType fallback lane, not the OSSYS BIGINT forcing.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every table's Id column; the shared blanket-BIGINT posture (vs deployed INT reality) is the estate-verification trigger the register keeps open
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: deploy an identifier column as INT; both pipelines emit BIGINT; V2 diagnostics additionally carry storageDivergence with emits=logical.

findings.topic: IDENTITY seed/increment + DBCC CHECKIDENT (pkt-c5)

v1.behavior: Seed/increment hard-coded 1/1 (identitySeed = isIdentity ? 1 : 0); the extraction contract selects is_identity only — never sys.identity_columns seed/increment; grep confirms zero DBCC CHECKIDENT/RESEED emission anywhere in src/.

v2.behavior: ColumnRealization.Identity IR channel exists (F10) but both OSSYS adapters set it to None, so emission always uses osNativeIdentity (1L,1L); no reader consults sys.identity_columns; zero DBCC emission; golden pins 'IDENTITY (1, 1)'; both sides bracket seed loads with SET IDENTITY_INSERT ON/OFF.

findings.difference: Claim pkt-c5 CONFIRMED in full on current code: both pipelines emit IDENTITY (1, 1) unconditionally, neither reflects a deliberate non-default seed (an estate table reseeded to e.g. 1000000 loses that on a fresh build in BOTH), and neither emits DBCC CHECKIDENT — IDENTITY_INSERT brackets preserve explicit values so max-id continuation is implicit.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — matters only for external tables with deliberate non-(1,1) seeds on a fresh build
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: reseed a source table (DBCC CHECKIDENT ... RESEED 5000), export with both; both emit IDENTITY (1, 1); after loading data with IDENTITY_INSERT the next identity continues from max loaded id on both.

findings.topic: Deployed/external 'numeric' base type rendering

v1.behavior: onDisk/external 'numeric' rules use the Decimal strategy, collapsing NUMERIC(p,s) to DECIMAL(p,s) in emitted DDL.

v2.behavior: SqlStorageType.Numeric is a distinct variant; deployed/external numeric renders as NUMERIC(p,s) via SqlDataTypeOption.Numeric.

findings.difference: A deployed NUMERIC column emits DECIMAL under V1 and NUMERIC under V2. SQL Server semantics are identical (numeric==decimal), but the type-name byte differs and DacFx schema compare treats them as equivalent-but-distinct declarations — diff noise between the two outputs.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — external/DBA numeric columns only; OutSystems native decimals map to decimal on both
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: deploy a NUMERIC(10,2) column; V1 emits DECIMAL (10, 2), V2 emits NUMERIC (10, 2).

findings.topic: Decimal with declared precision 0

v1.behavior: ResolvePrecision guards value > 0, so precision 0/negative falls back to 18 -> DECIMAL(18,0).

v2.behavior: OssysTypeMapping uses Option.defaultValue 18 precision — Some 0 passes through -> Decimal(0,0); dataTypeReferenceFromStorage renders whatever the storage carries (no >0 guard on the storage lane; only the PrimitiveType-fallback lane guards p>0).

findings.difference: An OSSYS decimal attribute carrying Precision=0 (instead of NULL) yields DECIMAL(18,0) in V1 but invalid DECIMAL(0, 0) DDL in V2 (deploy/DacFx build failure — loud, at least). Whether any real estate stores 0 rather than NULL is unverified.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — depends on estates persisting Precision=0 in ossys_Entity_Attr; unobserved in fixtures
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: rowset seed with a Decimal attribute Precision=0, Scale=0; V1 emits DECIMAL (18, 0); V2 emits DECIMAL (0, 0) and the DacFx build step should fail.

findings.topic: PK column NOT NULL forcing

v1.behavior: S1_PK signal makes every IsIdentifier column NOT NULL regardless of Is_Mandatory, in all modes (policy decisions always computed in step 3).

v2.behavior: No PK guard at emission: Nullable = keepNullable || (Column.IsNullable && not enforceNotNull) and Column.IsNullable = not Is_Mandatory; the PrimaryKey EnforceNotNull rule exists only inside the config-unreachable EvidenceDriven pass.

findings.difference: A pathological estate row with Is_Identifier=1 but Is_Mandatory=0 emits [Id] ... NULL PRIMARY KEY under V2 (deploy fails loudly with the SQL Server nullable-PK error) where V1 silently emits NOT NULL. Platform data normally guarantees mandatory identifiers, so this is a robustness nuance, not a routine difference.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — requires corrupted/unusual OSSYS catalog flags
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: rowset seed with Is_Identifier=1, Is_Mandatory=0; V1 emits NOT NULL; V2 emits NULL + inline PRIMARY KEY and the ScriptDom/DacFx validation of the bundle fails.

findings.topic: Unrecognized external dbType base name

v1.behavior: Unknown external base types fall to the default rule -> NVARCHAR(MAX) (parameters consulted for length via the ForExternal request).

v2.behavior: SqlStorageType.ofSqlType returns None for an unknown base name and resolveAttributeType keeps the OSSYS-derived logical storage (no override applied, no diagnostic).

findings.difference: An external dbType like 'geography' emits NVARCHAR(MAX) under V1 but the attribute's logical mapping (e.g. NVARCHAR(2000)->MAX or INT) under V2 — both are guesses; V2's is closer to the model, V1's is uniform. Neither refuses.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — external entities with CLR/spatial/exotic column types
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: attribute with ExternalColumnType='geography'; compare the emitted column type between pipelines.

findings.topic: SSDT_REMEDIATION_HANDOFF.md campaign state contradicts code

v1.behavior: n/a (doc-vs-code finding on the V2 sidecar's own campaign ledger).

v2.behavior: The handoff doc states 'Landed: ... WP-1a ... Not started: WP-1(b-e) and WP-2 ... WP-17', but the tree contains landed WP-1b (chooseOnDeleteAction), WP-3, WP-4, WP-4b, and WP-17(a-f), each with DECISIONS 2026-07-16 entries and register 'Landed' annotations.

findings.difference: Doc drift: the remediation campaign's state-of-record understates what has landed by roughly seven work packages in the types dimension alone. An operator triaging from the handoff would re-plan already-landed work; the review packet and SCALAR_REPRESENTATION_AUDIT are current, the handoff is not.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the handoff is the campaign's operator-facing entry point
findings.empiricalCheck: Static: compare SSDT_REMEDIATION_HANDOFF.md section 'Landed/Not started' against DECISIONS.md 2026-07-16 entries and the register's Landed lines.

findings.topic: CDC change-detection predicate exists in the engine but is never active on the production publish path

v1.behavior: MERGE WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET is unconditional on every matched row; no CDC awareness anywhere

v2.behavior: WHEN MATCHED gets the null-asymmetric change-detection predicate only when args.CdcAware=true, i.e. the kind is in Profile.CdcAwareness.CdcEnabled — but the publish path hardcodes Profile.empty.CdcAwareness ('CdcAwareness is never populated on the publish path (no ProfileDerivation / LiveProfiler axis writes it)') and LiveProfiler never reads sys.tables.is_tracked_by_cdc, so every production MERGE is emitted with the same unconditional UPDATE arm as V1

findings.difference: V2's flagship 'structurally CDC-silent redeploy' property (packet F2, driver stakes row 1) is a latent capability: the predicate machinery (ScriptDomBuild.fs:965-1009 perColumnChangeDetection, phase-2 diff predicate 1950-2018, staged UPDATE-from-temp WHERE 1389-1398) is real and canary-tested, but no production writer populates CdcAwareness — only ProfileCodec JSON readback (Targets.Json/ProfileCodec.fs:406-409) and tests construct it. The golden master StaticSeeds.sql contains zero 'WHEN MATCHED AND' predicates. On a real CDC-tracked estate a V2 publish redeploy churns CDC exactly as V1 does.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the SSIS consumer reads CDC; idempotent-redeploy CDC noise is the exact axis the cutover KPI names
findings.empiricalCheck: live lane: publish against localhost:11433 with a CDC-enabled table in scope; grep the emitted Data/*.sql for 'WHEN MATCHED AND' (expect absent); redeploy the seeds twice and count cdc.change_tables rows (expect non-zero)

findings.topic: V1's CDC redeploy leak confirmed, but the claimed 4-entry mechanism misattributes V1's phased generator

v1.behavior: Two real leak sites: (a) static seeds — WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET unconditionally rewrites every matched row on every redeploy (≈2 CDC entries/row); (b) bootstrap phase-2 — one set-based UPDATE Target SET fk=Source.fk FROM ... JOIN SourceRows re-writes every deferred-FK row unconditionally per bootstrap run. V1's phase-1 MERGE is INSERT-ONLY (WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT, no WHEN MATCHED arm) so it never overwrites deferred columns to NULL on rerun

v2.behavior: When CdcAware: WHEN MATCHED AND (col<>col OR null-asymmetry) predicate; deferred columns excluded from the UPDATE arm entirely (the '4 CDC entries/row' counterfactual in the docstring describes what V2 would do if it included them, since V2's phase-1 MERGE does have an UPDATE arm); phase-2 UPDATEs carry a per-cell difference predicate; staged phase-2 carries the shared change-detect WHERE

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f2's direction is right (V1 leaks structurally, V2 can be silent) but its V1 mechanism is wrong: V1's PhasedDynamicEntityInsertGenerator phase-1 has no UPDATE arm, so 'Phase-1 overwrites deferred column to NULL' never happens in V1; the 4-entries-per-row figure is V2's own design counterfactual (MergeRender.fs:51-57). V1's actual per-redeploy leak is 2 entries/matched static row (seeds) plus 2 entries/deferred row when bootstrap re-runs (normally guarded to first deploy only by the PostDeployment template).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — but only once CdcAwareness is wired (see data-lanes-cdc-predicate-unwired-on-publish)
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: construct Profile with CdcAwareness carrying a kind; emit and diff WHEN MATCHED arms; live lane: CdcSilenceCrossEmitter tests on IsolatedContainerFixture already pin C0-C4

findings.topic: IDENTITY_INSERT bracket: one GO batch (v2) vs three GO batches (v1)

v1.behavior: SET IDENTITY_INSERT ON; GO — MERGE; GO — SET IDENTITY_INSERT OFF; GO (three separate batches, both in seeds and in the phased bootstrap)

v2.behavior: SET IDENTITY_INSERT ON + MERGE + SET IDENTITY_INSERT OFF rendered as ONE GO batch, explicitly because the leveled deploy executor opens a connection per GO and the toggle is session-scoped; golden Tier kind witnesses the single batch

findings.difference: Same loaded state under plain sqlcmd (one session spans batches); V1's shape silently breaks (error 544/8101) under any executor that opens a connection per batch, and leaves IDENTITY_INSERT dangling ON between batches if a mid-script failure aborts. V2's single-batch bracket is safe under both execution models. Claim pkt-f3 confirmed.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every OutSystems entity PK is IDENTITY, so virtually every seeded kind brackets
findings.empiricalCheck: grep both outputs for the bracket shape; deploy v1's seed file through a per-batch-connection executor and observe the failure

findings.topic: Bootstrap lane semantics: convergent upsert MERGE (v2) vs insert-only MERGE (v1)

v1.behavior: Bootstrap phase-1 MERGEs are WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT only — an existing row is never touched; re-running bootstrap over drifted data preserves the target's values (except the unconditional phase-2 FK UPDATE); the PostDeployment template additionally guards the whole bootstrap to first deployment via IF NOT EXISTS on the first static table

v2.behavior: BootstrapEmitter delegates to the StaticSeedsEmitter MERGE renderer (A40 'same algebra'), so Data/Bootstrap.sql carries full upsert MERGEs — WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE + WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (delete arm suppressed); golden data-lanes Bootstrap.sql shows the UPDATE arm

findings.difference: Re-running the bootstrap artifact over an already-populated/drifted database converges the data to the source snapshot in V2 (overwriting any target-side edits) but is a no-op for existing rows in V1. V2 is idempotence-correct; for an operator used to V1's 'bootstrap never clobbers', a V2 bootstrap re-run is a data-overwrite event — and with CdcAwareness unwired it also rewrites every matched row (CDC/log churn at estate scale).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the first-deploy full snapshot is the cutover artifact; its re-run semantics matter operationally
findings.empiricalCheck: live lane: apply Bootstrap.sql twice against localhost:11433 with a hand-edited row in between; V2 output reverts the edit, V1 output preserves it

findings.topic: Bootstrap delivery: first-deploy-guarded post-deploy include (v1) vs explicit operator-run step excluded from post-deploy (v2)

v1.behavior: Emits Bootstrap/AllEntitiesIncludingStatic.bootstrap.sql plus a PostDeployment-Bootstrap.sql template whose IF NOT EXISTS([first static table]) guard :r-includes the bootstrap once, then :r-includes baseline seeds on every deployment (template is copy-into-project, with a non-deterministic generated-at timestamp)

v2.behavior: Data/Bootstrap.sql is deliberately filtered OUT of Script.PostDeployment.sql (post-deploy carries StaticSeeds + MigrationData only); bootstrap is a separate post-publish load the operator (or the store-leg leveled deploy) runs explicitly; no first-deploy guard is emitted anywhere

findings.difference: V1's (fragile) auto-once mechanism — guard keyed on one arbitrary static table being empty — has no V2 counterpart; the receiving pipeline must add the bootstrap step and its exactly-once discipline itself (packet F8; Estate.fs:1517 names it a manual gate). Operationally safer (no heuristic guard) but a net-new manual step in the cutover runbook.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — a cutover team following V1's deploy shape will silently never load bootstrap data
findings.empiricalCheck: emit with emission.sqlproj=true and inspect Script.PostDeployment.sql includes vs V1's PostDeployment-Bootstrap.sql

findings.topic: Platform user rows (ossys_User) in bootstrap: injected by default in v1, absent by default in v2

v1.behavior: SupplementalModelOptions.Default has IncludeUsers=true; the built-in ossys-user.json supplemental model (or OutSystemsInternalModel.Users fallback) is injected, and the bootstrap step queries live ossys_User rows via the static data provider and emits them into the renamed [dbo].[User] target inside AllEntitiesIncludingStatic.bootstrap.sql

v2.behavior: No supplemental-model injection exists; user rows would reach Data/Bootstrap.sql only if OSSYS_USER is a catalog kind, but the config default model.includeSystemModules=false excludes system entities from extraction; Reference.IsUserFk is never set true by any adapter (constructor default false; OssysJsonReader comment), so the user-remap discovery in both data emitters is inert; the supported user path is transfer --reconcile OSSYS_USER:EMAIL

findings.difference: A default V2 publish bundle carries no platform-user rows (and no User kind), while V1's default bundle seeds dbo.User in bootstrap. Any migrated table with CreatedBy/UpdatedBy-style FKs to the user table loads against an unseeded target in V2 unless the operator either flips includeSystemModules (which drags in ALL system entities under their ossys_ names) or runs the transfer/reconcile leg. Confirms and extends claim pkt-e9 (UserReflow near-inert; V1's multi-env+UAT-users+user-reflow cluster deferred as a unit).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every OutSystems estate has ossys_User and user-audit FKs
findings.empiricalCheck: live lane: publish against a warm OSSYS fixture DB on localhost:11433 with default config; grep Data/Bootstrap.sql for the user table (expect absent); compare V1 full-export Bootstrap file (expect dbo.User MERGE present)

findings.topic: V1 Authoritative mode + >1000 rows: per-batch DELETE arm destroys other batches' rows; V2 staged form is structurally immune

v1.behavior: Rows >1000 partition into successive ≤1000-row MERGEs, and in Authoritative mode EACH batch's MERGE carries an unscoped WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE — batch N deletes every row not in batch N's 1000-row source, including rows batches 1..N-1 just inserted; final state ≈ last batch only

v2.behavior: Above the staging threshold the ENTIRE row set stages into one #temp and a single MERGE ... USING [#temp] carries the (scoped) delete arm, so the delete arm always sees the complete source; module docstring names this as a correctness property of staging ('the DELETE arm + two-phase + guard stay correct precisely because the whole source lands in one #temp')

findings.difference: Latent data-loss bug in V1 for any static entity with >1000 rows under Authoritative synchronization (default is NonDestructive, so only opt-in configs are exposed); V2 cannot express the bug — delete arms ride a single full-source MERGE.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only estates that opted into V1 Authoritative mode on large static entities
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: build a 2500-row static kind, emit V1 with Authoritative and apply to localhost:11433; count surviving rows (expect ≈ last batch); apply V2 with deleteScope and expect all 2500

findings.topic: Convergent delete: scoped + signoff-gated arm (v2) vs unscoped Authoritative mode (v1)

v1.behavior: StaticSeedSynchronizationMode.Authoritative emits an UNSCOPED 'WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE THEN DELETE;' — whole-table convergence, no gate beyond picking the mode; default NonDestructive emits no arm

v2.behavior: emission.deleteScope emits 'WHEN NOT MATCHED BY SOURCE AND [Target].[col]=<value> ... THEN DELETE' only on kinds where every term column resolves against the post-chain catalog attributes (case-insensitive realized-column match); REFUSED (emission.deleteScope.ungreenlit) unless emission.signoff carries mode delete-scope; empty terms list is a config refusal so no unscoped variant exists; Bootstrap lane suppresses the arm unconditionally

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f5 confirmed (signoff gate, scoped arm, bootstrap-never-deletes; golden ScopedLookup line 30 witnesses). Two refinements: (1) the Policy.fs docstring still says terms name PHYSICAL columns while resolution matches the post-chain realized (renamed) column — the packet's 'stale docs' finding is confirmed in code at Policy.fs:64 itself; (2) new V2 trap: a term value of "" authors NULL (Policy.fs:85-89), and the arm renders '[Target].[col] = NULL' — always UNKNOWN under ANSI_NULLS, so that delete arm can never fire (a silently-dead scope). V1's unscoped whole-table convergence has no V2 equivalent (deliberate capability removal).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — tenant-scoped static data cleanup at cutover
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: emit with deleteScope term value "" and inspect the rendered predicate ('= NULL'); apply to localhost:11433 and verify no rows delete

findings.topic: Drift guard: symmetric-EXCEPT THROW parity, docstring contradiction confirmed, staged form airtight

v1.behavior: ValidateThenApply emits IF EXISTS(target) AND (EXISTS(src EXCEPT tgt) OR EXISTS(tgt EXCEPT src)) THROW 50000 as its OWN GO batch before the MERGE (and an IF EXISTS THROW for empty-row tables)

v2.behavior: emission.dataVerification=validateBeforeApply prepends the typed parse-template render of the same symmetric-EXCEPT guard; INLINE form: guard + '\nGO\n' + MERGE batch — the guard is its own GO batch, contradicting the DataVerification docstring ('one GO batch with the MERGE'); STAGED form: guard is an inner statement of the single SET XACT_ABORT ON TRY/TRAN batch — a tripped guard rolls back the whole unit

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f6 confirmed exactly: inline V2 shares V1's weakness (plain sqlcmd without -b continues past the THROW into the MERGE batch), the docstring is wrong (code wins), and the staged form is strictly stronger than anything V1 had. One V1-only edge V2 drops: V1 guards empty-row tables (IF EXISTS THROW when source has zero rows); V2 renders nothing for empty loads so emptiness drift is unguarded.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only operators choosing the conservative validateBeforeApply posture
findings.empiricalCheck: emit a kind with dataVerification=validateBeforeApply at both sub- and supra-threshold row counts; count GO separators between guard and MERGE (inline: 1; staged: 0)

findings.topic: Large-kind handling: #temp staging with atomic envelope + measured index threshold (v2) vs 1000-row MERGE batching with PRINT (v1)

v1.behavior: Rows >1000: partition into ≤1000-row MERGE statements each preceded by PRINT 'Applying batch i/n...'; no transaction, no #temp; the delete-arm interaction is broken (see authoritative-batching finding); the inline-MERGE plan-complexity wall is dodged only by the batch split

v2.behavior: DataStagingPolicy: auto stages >1000 rows through #seed_<table> (chunked 1000-row INSERTs — the VALUES-constructor cap — then ONE MERGE USING #temp) inside one SET XACT_ABORT ON TRY/TRAN GO-free batch; >100k adds a clustered #temp PK index (measured 33-37% faster, no-PK kinds skip it); phase-2 escalates to one UPDATE...FROM #fk_<table> at the same threshold; inline sub-threshold MERGEs deliberately carry no explicit transaction (idempotent-rerun recovery); modes inline/tempTable pin the posture

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f7 confirmed in full (thresholds, 8623 rationale, atomicity, index measurement, inline-no-transaction recovery model). V1's equivalent knob (batch splitting) trades atomicity for nothing and floods the log with per-batch MERGEs; V2's staged unit is all-or-nothing and faster at scale.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — real estates have >1000-row static/lookup tables
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: emit a 5000-row kind and inspect the staged shape; live lane: apply to localhost:11433 and kill mid-batch, verify rollback leaves zero partial rows

findings.topic: Empty-string-vs-NULL fidelity: the claimed universal ''→NULL sentinel is retired (WP-3 landed)

v1.behavior: Preserves N''; the only coercion is the deliberate OutSystems single-space sentinel — a 1-char " " string on a nullable column reads back as NULL, applied at every data-provider read (static, dynamic, supplemental)

v2.behavior: WP-3 (DECISIONS 2026-07-16): cells are string option end-to-end — None is SQL NULL out-of-band, Some "" is a genuine value (Text renders N'', Binary 0x); an empty raw on any other type refuses loudly (rawValue.empty.notEmptyCapable); the EmptyTextNormalizedToNull tolerance is deleted from Tolerance.fs; the single-space sentinel is scoped to the static-seed/bootstrap render lanes only (Type=Text ∧ not mandatory), never transfer or migration; golden TextFidelity witnesses (1,N''),(2,NULL),(3,NULL←sentinel),(4,N'hello')

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f11 is STALE — fixed on main 2026-07-16 (packet F11 itself is marked ✅ WP-3 LANDED). Residual deliberate ''=NULL conventions survive only at config-parse boundaries (migration-dependencies file values, delete-scope term values), where a genuine empty string is not expressible. Sentinel scope nuance: V1 applies it value-driven on ANY nullable column at read; V2 requires the attribute type be Text — equivalent for real OutSystems data. V2's bootstrap lane applies the sentinel (it delegates to the static renderer), matching V1's bootstrap-read behavior.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — OutSystems Text semantics make ''-vs-NULL load-bearing
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: round-trip a row with '' and NULL cells through emit+apply on localhost:11433 and read back; verify distinct storage

findings.topic: MERGE VALUES row ordering: typed PK sort (v1) vs row-Identifier SsKey sort with lexicographic-index scrambling on the live path (v2)

v1.behavior: EntitySeedDeterminizer sorts rows by PK columns with type-aware comparison (numeric via decimal coercion, DateTime/Guid/bool/byte[] dispatch, ordinal-string fallback, whole-row tiebreak, array-length tertiary); SQL read is ORDER BY all PK columns

v2.behavior: NormalizeStaticPopulations sorts by (row.Identifier SsKey, Values map); on the live path the identifier is the synthesized READSIDE_ROW '<schema>.<table>.<rowIdx>' minted in acquisition order, acquisition is ORDER BY the FIRST PK column only, and SsKey's structural comparison is ordinal-string — so '...10' sorts before '...2', reordering any kind with ≥11 rows into lexicographic-index order (0,1,10,11,...,2,20,...); test-authored rows sort by their authored record-name identifiers (golden Tier renders Bronze,Gold,Silver = PKs 1,3,2)

findings.difference: Both deterministic per-input, but neither matches the other and V2's live order is neither PK order nor SELECT order beyond 10 rows (claim pkt-f2's 'PK-first deterministic row order' and adm-seed-determinizer's 'V1 behavioral contract satisfied' are inaccurate for the current live path; the named differential witnesses — StaticAdapterDifferentialTests.fs and the emission/edge-case seed fixture — no longer exist in the tree). Additional determinism edge: a composite-PK kind's acquisition ties (single-column ORDER BY) are DB-unspecified, so index-minted identity can vary across runs → potential byte-instability for composite-PK static/bootstrap kinds. MERGE semantics are order-independent, so the loaded database is unaffected.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes for review/diff workflows (byte-diff vs V1 seeds guaranteed to differ); composite-PK edge is conditional (rare in OutSystems)
findings.empiricalCheck: live lane: hydrate a 15-row static kind from localhost:11433 and inspect Data/StaticSeeds.sql VALUES order (expect 1,10,11,...,2,...); repeat twice on a composite-PK kind to probe stability

findings.topic: Lane architecture: three disjoint per-lane files with asserted partition (v2) vs overlapping Seeds+Bootstrap files (v1)

v1.behavior: Static rows appear TWICE by design: Seeds/<Module>/StaticEntities.seed.sql (every deploy) AND inside Bootstrap/AllEntitiesIncludingStatic.bootstrap.sql (static+regular+supplemental fused in one global topological file, first-deploy-guarded); no migration channel exists

v2.behavior: Three lanes (StaticSeeds/MigrationData/Bootstrap) with a hard partition assertion — any kind populated by two lanes under one DataComposition fails compose with OverlappingEmitterCoverage; Bootstrap defaults to the complement (AllRemaining); emission.bootstrapAllData=true flips to AllData (Bootstrap covers everything, siblings dispatched empty — the V1-shaped full snapshot); the fused Data/seed.sql is retired (perLaneFiles are the artifacts; composeRenderedFull remains on-demand)

findings.difference: Claims pkt-f1 and dec-fused-seed-retired confirmed (tests assert Data/seed.sql absent — FullExportDataBundleTests.fs:72-74). Two notes: (1) V2's default first deploy loads static rows via the StaticSeeds lane only, V1's loads them via bootstrap then re-MERGEs via seeds — same end state, different file contents; V1 parity of file shape requires bootstrapAllData=true. (2) The named doc drift is real and survives in code comments — Pipeline.fs:810 still says 'adds them to the bundle as Data/seed.sql'.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — reviewed/applied artifact set differs from V1's
findings.empiricalCheck: bundle publish and ls Data/ (expect up to three files, no seed.sql); configure a static kind in the migration file and expect OverlappingEmitterCoverage under AllRemaining

findings.topic: Two-phase FK-cycle insertion: predicate parity with three deltas (DB-constraint filter, self-loops, refusal site)

v1.behavior: Defers a column iff relationship.HasDatabaseConstraint ∧ target-in-cycle ∧ !IsMandatory; single-member SCCs are excluded from the cycle set (component.Length<2), so self-FKs are never deferred (a single insert-only MERGE satisfies them intra-statement); phase-1 NULLs via CASE WHEN 1=0 CTE projection; phase-2 is one set-based UPDATE...JOIN VALUES CTE per table; NOT-NULL cycles just emit in whatever order the (possibly alphabetical-fallback) sorter produced and fail at deploy

v2.behavior: Defers iff kind-in-cycle ∧ target-in-same-cycle ∧ column nullable — NO HasDbConstraint filter on the reference; self-loops are cycle members (per-kind Rendered is self-FK-correct; composer's global Phase-1-then-Phase-2 ordering handles multi-kind cycles); phase-1 emits NULL literals in VALUES; phase-2 is per-row UPDATEs below the staging threshold, one UPDATE...FROM #fk above; non-nullable same-cycle FKs surface as UnbreakableCycleFk — refused on the TRANSFER leg (transfer.load.unsatisfiablePlan) but only diagnosed, not refused, on the emit/publish leg

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f4 confirmed with refinements: (1) V2 also defers nullable FKs that carry no database constraint (harmless extra phase-2 but emitted SQL differs from V1); (2) nullable self-FK kinds get NULL+UPDATE in V2 vs single-statement real values in V1 (same final state; different statement stream and first-deploy CDC shape); (3) 'NOT-NULL cycles are a named refusal' is transfer-leg-only — the published Bootstrap/Seeds artifacts still render and would fail at apply, matching V1's deploy-time failure but with a diagnostic. Also the parity-matrix test skip text (row 160: 'global ordering NOT YET REIFIED') is stale — slice ι reified it in the composer.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — OutSystems models routinely carry nullable cyclic refs without DB constraints (deleteRule=Ignore)
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: author a 2-kind nullable cycle with HasDbConstraint=false and diff the deferral behavior; author a NOT-NULL cycle and confirm publish emits while transfer refuses

findings.topic: Operator-curated migration-dependency channel is v2-only

v1.behavior: No equivalent: the only data sources are static entities (model+provider), the extracted dynamic dataset, and supplemental models; no operator-authored row file

v2.behavior: overrides.migrationDependencies.path supplies logical-keyed operator rows (Module/Entity resolved to SsKey against the catalog); '' and JSON null both parse to None=NULL (a genuine empty string is documented as not expressible); rows ride the same DataLoadPlan/MERGE algebra with all three optional axes; naming a kind excludes the WHOLE kind from the Bootstrap complement (operator owns completeness)

findings.difference: Claim pkt-f9 confirmed in code (kind-level bootstrap exclusion at the hydration complement; composite-PK ON clause witnessed in golden MigrationData.sql). Net-new capability with one sharp edge (whole-kind exclusion means a partial migration file silently unloads the rest of that kind from bootstrap — by documented design).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — legacy-domain reference rows at cutover
findings.empiricalCheck: emit with a migrationDependencies file naming one kind; verify the kind appears only in Data/MigrationData.sql and is absent from Data/Bootstrap.sql

findings.topic: Seed-row sourcing: fixture JSON or live SQL (v1) vs live-connection-only hydration (v2)

v1.behavior: IStaticEntityDataProvider has both a FixtureStaticEntityDataProvider (JSON tables file, offline) and SqlStaticEntityDataProvider (live, ORDER BY PKs); a model with static entities REQUIRES a provider (hard failure otherwise); dynamic dataset likewise fixture- or extraction-sourced

v2.behavior: WP6 hydration streams rows only from model.ossys (live connection) via Ingestion/ReadSide; the OSM model read marks static kinds Static [] (populations empty); the static-entities.*.json importer was removed 2026-06-08; a model.path-only run emits empty data lanes with the NAMED diagnostic data.hydration.skippedStaticNoOssysConnection; V1_PARITY_MATRIX row 165 records the fixture-provider pattern as NOT integrated

findings.difference: Claim rec-data-lanes-hydration confirmed (hydration+delegation landed; empty pre-WP6 shape survives as degenerate case). The refinement: V1's offline/fixture-sourced seed emission has no V2 production equivalent — an air-gapped or model-only export cannot produce populated data lanes (named skip, not silent). Also V2 fails soft (empty lanes + diagnostic) where V1 fails hard (missingProvider error).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — matters for offline bundle production; live cutover runs have the connection
findings.empiricalCheck: run publish with model.path only and confirm Data/ lanes absent plus the named diagnostic in the run report

findings.topic: Pipelined (acquisition-overlapped) bootstrap publish arm — schedule-only toggle

v1.behavior: No equivalent: dynamic extraction fully materializes the dataset (paged OFFSET/FETCH), then the bootstrap file renders in a later step

v2.behavior: emission.pipelinedBootstrap (default true) renders each eligible kind's MERGE and derives its profile evidence on the drain worker as rows land (BootstrapLane.Prerendered), capping live-row memory at dataReadConcurrency kinds; false keeps the two-phase drain-then-render schedule; both arms must produce identical artifacts (same renderLoad/loadFor core; delete-scope suppressed; equivalence pinned by test per docstring)

findings.difference: Claim dec-pipelined-bootstrap confirmed ('the schedule changes, the bundle does not'). Memory posture at estate scale is structurally better than V1's full-estate materialization. Note the drain-time render bakes in the same empty CdcAwareness (Pipeline.fs:2221-2225) — consistent with the compose-time render, which is what makes the byte-equality hold.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — whole-estate row acquisition is the publish bottleneck
findings.empiricalCheck: publish twice with pipelinedBootstrap true/false against localhost:11433 and byte-diff the bundle (expect identical)

findings.topic: SQL literal rendering: WP-17 closed the temporal/control-char/LOB gaps to V1 parity, with residual micro-deltas

v1.behavior: N'..' with '' doubling and CR/LF/TAB → ' + CHAR(n) + N' splices; CAST temporal forms (datetime2(7)/date/time(7)/datetimeoffset(7)); TimeSpan renders bare '{ts:c}' WITHOUT CAST; Guid quoted D-form; binary 0x-hex (empty → 0x); bool 1/0; invariant numerics with double G17/float G9; unknown types fall back to N'ToString()'

v2.behavior: SqlLiteral DU: TextLit with segment-spliced CHAR() escapes (exactly V1's 13/10/9 set), DateTimeLit/DateLit/TimeLit/DateTimeOffsetLit as V1's CAST forms (WP-17(b)/(d), offset-bearing raws own datetimeoffset(7)), GuidLit quoted, BinaryLit 0x, BooleanLit 1/0, Integer/Decimal raw digits; comparison-less types (xml/text/ntext/image) get MAX-cast change-detect compares (WP-17(c)); malformed raws refuse loudly instead of ToString-fallback

findings.difference: Substantive parity landed 2026-07-16 (WP-17). Residual deltas: V2 always CASTs time literals where V1's TimeSpan path emitted a bare quoted string (same stored value; V2 language-independent); V1's float/double G17/G9 and unknown-type fallbacks have no V2 counterpart (V2's typed IR refuses instead — safer); byte-level formatting differs everywhere else only via ScriptDom rendering (aliases bracketed, single-line VALUES).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — literal fidelity is the row-value carriage for every seeded cell
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: seed a kind covering all scalar types and diff literal shapes against a V1 seed of the same rows

findings.topic: Per-module seed files with banners/PRINTs/timestamps (v1) vs three bare per-lane files (v2)

v1.behavior: Seeds/<Module>/StaticEntities.seed.sql per module (GroupByModule default true; master file opt-in; module-name sanitize/dedup), each wrapped in a banner template + SET NOCOUNT ON, per-entity comment headers (Module/Entity/Target), batch PRINT statements; bootstrap and post-deploy template headers embed DateTime.UtcNow (non-deterministic bytes every run); FK preflight (orphans/ordering) logged

v2.behavior: Exactly Data/StaticSeeds.sql / Data/MigrationData.sql / Data/Bootstrap.sql (empty lanes dropped), globally topo-ordered Phase-1-then-Phase-2, no banners, no comments, no PRINT, no SET NOCOUNT ON, no timestamps — byte-pinned against goldens; V1's orphan-row preflight is NOT carried (parity-matrix row 162 🟠 NOT-MAPPED; only TopologicalOrder.MissingEdges partial)

findings.difference: Layout and annotation regime entirely different: V2 trades V1's per-module review granularity and progress PRINTs for determinism and a global cycle-correct phase ordering V1's per-module files could not express. V1's non-deterministic timestamps make its bootstrap/post-deploy files un-diffable run-to-run; V2 is byte-stable. The dropped static-seed FK preflight (orphan detection with remediation guidance) is a real diagnostic capability gap, acknowledged in-tree.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — review workflow and CI diffing of the data artifacts
findings.empiricalCheck: publish twice and byte-diff V2 Data/ (identical) vs run V1 twice and diff Bootstrap header (differs)

findings.topic: Seed artifacts wired into the buildable project: derived-from-actual-lanes (v2) vs stale-enumeration ordering bug (v1)

v1.behavior: BuildSsdtSqlProjectStep enumerates OutputDirectory/Seeds for project items, but the pipeline runs the sqlproj step BEFORE the static-seed step, so on a fresh output the Seeds directory does not exist and the generated .sqlproj carries zero seed items (only a stale prior run's files would appear); the bootstrap file is likewise never a project item

v2.behavior: emission.sqlproj derives the lane set from the ACTUAL emitted DataBundle map, marks lanes Build-Remove, wires Script.PostDeployment.sql with :r includes for exactly the non-bootstrap lanes ('the post-deploy :r includes can never dangle')

findings.difference: V1 has a sequencing defect making its generated project ignore seeds on first run (and include phantom stale files on dirty re-runs); V2's derivation is structurally consistent with what was emitted.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only teams consuming the generated .sqlproj directly
findings.empiricalCheck: run V1 full-export into a clean directory and inspect the .sqlproj for seed items (expect none); run V2 with sqlproj:true and verify includes match Data/ contents

findings.topic: Deprecated dynamic-insert lane: v1 no-op step retained, v2 config concept deleted; INSERT-stream role reassigned

v1.behavior: BuildSsdtDynamicInsertStep is a hard no-op logging dynamicData.deprecated; the DynamicEntityInsertGenerator (INSERT WITH (TABLOCK), NOCHECK/CHECK CONSTRAINT ALL wrapping on cycles, per-row self-FK topological ordering, StaticSeedParentHandlingMode) is dead on the pipeline path — only the phased generator survives via the bootstrap snapshot

v2.behavior: No DynamicDataSection exists in Config.fs (grep-zero); InsertMode concerns live in Policy.InsertionPolicy (SchemaOnly/InsertNew/Merge DU); StaticSeedParentMode not carried; junction deferral lives in the ordering axis; the fresh-deploy INSERT-stream role is StaticPopulationEmitter (typed Statement.InsertRow stream for canary/bulk realizations, honoring column-subset omission and the space sentinel) — not a bundle artifact

findings.difference: Claim dec-dynamicdata-struck confirmed: V2 deleted a concept V1 itself deprecated; nothing an operator uses on the V1 default path is lost. V1's constraint-disabling fallback (NOCHECK ALL on cycle detection) is deliberately not reproduced — V2's two-phase pattern replaces it without ever disabling constraints.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: no — the lane was already dead in v1's full-export
findings.empiricalCheck: grep v2 Config.fs for DynamicData (absent); run v1 full-export and confirm the deprecated log line

findings.topic: Row identity for PK-less kinds: acknowledged-override fallback in both, with different fallback column sets

v1.behavior: No PK → hard error unless allowMissingPrimaryKey override; fallback matches ON ALL columns (identity and updatable included), which also makes the WHEN MATCHED arm vacuous (matched ⇒ identical)

v2.behavior: No PK → matchAttributes falls back to writable columns EXCLUDING computed, identity, and deferred columns (identity can't re-identify; deferred would mis-join the Phase-1 NULL row); the fallback empties the UPDATE arm (buildMergeStatement skips it when UpdColumns empty); unacknowledged kinds fail earlier at the diagnostics gate; explicitly scoped as data-movement row identity, not a schema claim

findings.difference: Same operator contract (acknowledge missing PK or refuse) and same net idempotent-insert semantics; V2's exclusion of identity/deferred columns from the match set fixes two correctness corners V1's all-columns match has (an IDENTITY column in the ON clause can never match a re-seeded value; a deferred column would never re-join). Emitted ON-clauses differ for such kinds.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — PK-less tables are rare, acknowledged populations
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: emit an acknowledged no-PK kind with an identity column in both systems and compare ON clauses and redeploy behavior

findings.topic: Transfer-leg FK re-trust after bulk load: default on, streaming/synthetic legs not wired

v1.behavior: No comparable bulk-load transfer surface in full-export (schema apply path exists but the reverse-leg bulk loader is a v2-only capability)

v2.behavior: WriteOptions.def.RetrustForeignKeys = true; Execute re-validates untrusted FKs post-load (WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT) with retrust/retrust-skipped stage telemetry; opting out is the named FkTrustNotRestoredOnBulkLoad tolerance; Tolerance.fs itself documents that writePlanStreaming and runSynthetic do not yet wire the re-trust step ('a named Wave-2 follow-on')

findings.difference: Claim pkt-e10 confirmed exactly as stated (default path re-trusts; decision-untrusted FKs stay untrusted; streaming/synthetic legs always exhibit the tolerance until WP-15). v2-only surface — no v1 counterpart to regress against.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — big-load reverse-leg runs; runbook carries the manual sweep until WP-15
findings.empiricalCheck: live lane: run a streaming transfer into localhost:11433 and query sys.foreign_keys.is_not_trusted (expect 1 until WP-15)

findings.topic: Representation-only tolerances CharAnsiPadding + DecimalScale name SQL Server semantics; change-detect compares stored values

v1.behavior: No change-detection predicate exists, so the question does not arise; V1's unconditional UPDATE rewrites regardless

v2.behavior: CharAnsiPaddingTolerated and DecimalScaleTolerated documented as representation-only: the predicate compares Target.[c] <> Source.[c] column-to-column on stored typed values (ANSI-padded char equality; numeric scale equality), so padding/scale text differences never fire CDC; comparison-less types dispatch through MAX-cast compares

findings.difference: Claim pkt-char-decimal-tolerances confirmed against the predicate construction; genuinely closes the equivalence at the literal/predicate level rather than assuming it.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — char(n)/decimal static columns under CDC
findings.empiricalCheck: live lane CDC canary with padded char values (IsolatedContainerFixture) — already scripted in the v2 suite

findings.topic: Zero-row kinds: comment placeholder (v1) vs total omission (v2)

v1.behavior: Emits the entity banner plus '-- No data rows were returned for this static entity; MERGE statement omitted.' (and, under ValidateThenApply, an IF EXISTS(target) THROW guard so a target that HAS rows when the source has none still trips drift)

v2.behavior: Empty loads render the empty script; the kind contributes zero bytes to the lane file (T11 keyset preserved internally); no emptiness drift guard exists for zero-row sources

findings.difference: Cosmetic omission plus one real edge: under validateBeforeApply, V1 detects 'target has rows but source is empty' drift; V2 renders no guard for empty sources so that drift direction passes silently on the emit artifacts.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — static entity emptied at source after target seeded
findings.empiricalCheck: fixture lane: emit a zero-row kind with verification on in both systems; apply against a populated target and compare abort behavior

findings.topic: Delete-rule code mapping (OutSystems vocabulary)

v1.behavior: MapDeleteRule: null/whitespace->NoAction; 'Cascade'->Cascade; 'Delete'->Cascade; 'Protect'->NoAction; 'Ignore'->NoAction; 'SetNull'->SetNull; any other code SILENTLY -> NoAction (case-sensitive switch).

v2.behavior: parseDeleteRule: None->NoAction; 'Delete'->Cascade; 'Protect'->NoAction; 'Ignore'->NoAction; 'SetNull'->SetNull; any other code (including the literal 'Cascade' v1 accepted) is a HARD adapter error adapter.osm.unmappedDeleteRule.

findings.difference: Identical action table on the real OutSystems vocabulary (Delete/Protect/Ignore/SetNull/missing). On unknown codes v1 silently degrades to NO ACTION while v2 fails loud; v2 also rejects the literal 'Cascade' string v1 tolerated. WP-1d (DECISIONS 2026-07-16) removed the inert TreatMissingDeleteRuleAsIgnore knob from v2 config (claim dec-e1-unknown-code said 'inert' — now removed; Policy.fs:498-505).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — OutSystems only emits Delete/Protect/Ignore/null; unknown codes require a corrupted or future-platform model
findings.empiricalCheck: Feed a model JSON with reference_deleteRuleCode:'Cascade' through v2 fixture lane and expect adapter.osm.unmappedDeleteRule; same input through v1 full-export emits the FK with CASCADE

findings.topic: Reflected delete action on the evidence path (SQL Server vocabulary)

v1.behavior: ForeignKeyEvidenceResolver maps constraint.OnDeleteAction (sys.foreign_keys delete_referential_action_desc: 'CASCADE'/'NO_ACTION'/'SET_NULL', confirmed in fixture tests/Fixtures/model.edge-case.json:200) through the same case-sensitive MapDeleteRule whose cases are 'Cascade'/'SetNull' — so a deployed ON DELETE CASCADE or SET NULL FK maps to the default branch => NoAction, silently. The emitted FK loses its cascade/set-null action.

v2.behavior: Two separate parsers: parseSqlForeignKeyAction handles the SQL vocabulary ('NO_ACTION'/'CASCADE'/'SET_NULL'; 'SET_DEFAULT' degrades to None); chooseOnDeleteAction prefers the representable reflected action over the model rule (WP-1b), and deleteRuleDivergences names any representable disagreement as adapter.ossys.fkReality.deleteActionDivergence.

findings.difference: V1 has a latent case-sensitivity bug: any source-backed FK whose deployed action is CASCADE/SET_NULL re-emits from v1 full-export as plain (NO ACTION) whenever actual-constraint evidence is present (the usual case, since onDelete is always populated for reflected FKs); the only in-tree fixture uses NO_ACTION so v1's suite never catches it. V2 emits the deployed action faithfully and names model/reality disagreement. Cutover diffs will show ON DELETE CASCADE appearing in v2 output where v1 emitted none.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — OutSystems 'Delete' rules deploy as physical ON DELETE CASCADE constraints; every such FK is mis-emitted by v1
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane against localhost:11433: create an FK with ON DELETE CASCADE, run v1 full-export and v2 publish, diff the emitted FK's ON DELETE clause

findings.topic: ON UPDATE referential action

v1.behavior: UpdateAction extracted (outsystems_metadata_rowsets.sql:655, ForeignKeysResultSetProcessor.cs:10) and carried to RelationshipActualConstraint.OnUpdateAction / ForeignKeyModel.UpdateRule, but never consumed by emission: CreateTableStatementBuilder sets only constraint.DeleteAction (and only when != NoAction); no UpdateAction is ever set.

v2.behavior: Reference.OnUpdate (option) carried from #FkReality.UpdateAction via the toBundle JOIN, parsed by parseSqlForeignKeyAction, emitted via ScriptDom UpdateAction when Some; None omits the clause. Golden pins ON UPDATE CASCADE (with ON DELETE NO ACTION backfilled by the elegant formatter when only one clause is present).

findings.difference: V2 is a strict superset: deployed ON UPDATE actions survive into emitted DDL; v1 silently dropped them. SET_DEFAULT (both ON DELETE and ON UPDATE) is unrepresentable in v2's ReferenceAction DU: the clause degrades to model-rule/omitted, and deleteActionDivergence deliberately does NOT name the unrepresentable case (OssysTranslation.fs:470-475) — a small residual silent degrade on both sides.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — the platform does not author ON UPDATE actions; hand-added FKs on estate databases can carry them
findings.empiricalCheck: Deploy an FK with ON UPDATE CASCADE to warm SQL, run both exports, grep the emitted table file for ON UPDATE

findings.topic: Untrusted-FK (WITH NOCHECK) reproduction — final deployed state

v1.behavior: IsNoCheck FKs are skipped inline and deferred to: ALTER TABLE .. WITH NOCHECK ADD CONSTRAINT .. FOREIGN KEY .. (adds enabled+untrusted) followed by ALTER TABLE .. NOCHECK CONSTRAINT ..; (DISABLES it). Net deployed state: is_disabled=1 — the FK no longer enforces any DML.

v2.behavior: FK created inline (trusted), then the two-step: ALTER TABLE .. NOCHECK CONSTRAINT (disable) then ALTER TABLE .. WITH NOCHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT (re-enable skipping validation). Net state: is_not_trusted=1, is_disabled=0 — enabled but untrusted, order verified against SQL Server; pinned in golden.

findings.difference: Both source SQLs define IsNoCheck = sys.foreign_keys.is_not_trusted (outsystems_metadata_rowsets.sql:660, byte-equal copies). The source state is 'enabled, untrusted'. V2 reproduces it exactly; v1's second statement disables the constraint outright, so a v1-deployed target silently stops enforcing the FK for new rows — a semantic downgrade of the deployed database.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — estates accumulate untrusted FKs from bulk loads; reproducing them as disabled changes runtime enforcement
findings.empiricalCheck: Deploy v1's two-statement output and v2's golden Engagement.sql to warm SQL; compare sys.foreign_keys is_disabled/is_not_trusted for the FK

findings.topic: Logical-only reference materialization — defaults (register E2)

v1.behavior: FK emission requires a positive policy decision. Default TighteningOptions (EvidenceGated, EnableCreation=true, AllowCrossSchema=false, AllowNoCheckCreation=false) + mandatory profile stage: logical-only FKs are created only when no orphans observed and gates pass; orphaned/cross-schema logical refs are withheld with rationales and an opportunity record.

v2.behavior: With no foreignKey intervention registered (the config default), the emitter emits EVERY deployable reference as a real, trusted, enforced FK (including ON DELETE CASCADE) — no orphan check, no cross-schema gate; DropFk only populates from intervention decisions. The WP-1c(ii) eject regime (2026-07-16) makes the evidence-gated intervention config-mandatory for eject/production configs (not code-forced), restoring V1-strict behavior; the golden corpus re-recorded to the gated posture.
