findings.difference: The permissive emit-everything default remains v2's out-of-the-box behavior for configs that omit the intervention; the intended eject posture is opt-in-by-config, always registered in the shipped eject config. Claim pkt-e2's 'golden pins logical-only Cascade/SetNull FKs on Engagement' is now STALE: the re-recorded golden WITHHOLDS all four logical-only FKs (Engagement.Customer/AltCustomer, RegionA/B.Partner) and Engagement.sql carries no CustomerId FK.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — a real estate has hundreds of logical-only refs; forgetting the intervention materializes them all as enforced cascading FKs
findings.empiricalCheck: Run v2 publish with and without policy.tightening foreignKey intervention on the same source; diff emitted FK sets against v1 full-export output

findings.topic: FK intervention gate order + EvidenceMissing inversion (register E3)

v1.behavior: Evaluator order: hasConstraint => create; else EnableCreation && !orphan && !crossBlocked => create; Cautious mode + EnableCreation + (orphan||ignoreRule) + !crossBlocked => create WITH NOCHECK (applied only if AllowNoCheckCreation). Absence of profile rows silently counts as 'no orphans' (creation proceeds). Missing target silently yields no opportunity.

v2.behavior: Order: 0. OperatorUntracked override (absolute, outranks even source-backed carve-out) / RelaxationOnly direction => DeclaredShapeCarried; 1. MissingTarget; 2. HasDbConstraint => enforce regardless of remaining gates; 3. EnableCreation; 4. profile-driven: no/unreliable probe => EvidenceMissing => DROP; orphans+AllowNoCheckCreation => ScriptWithNoCheck; orphans => DataHasOrphans; clean+cross-schema => CrossSchemaBlocked. Every outcome has a named diagnostic (tightening.foreignKey.*, decision.fkDropped Warning for source-backed, decision.fkNotIntroduced Info for logical-only).

findings.difference: Confirmed with two post-claims extensions: (a) the 2026-07-15 estate A6 amendment added per-reference KeepUntracked overrides + RelaxationOnly direction — OperatorUntracked deliberately outranks the source-backed carve-out (amends rec-dropfk-carveout's 'source-backed always emit'); (b) the inversion stands: intervention ON + no live profile drops ALL logical-only FKs (EvidenceMissing) — stricter than no intervention at all (which emits all), the opposite of v1 where absent evidence permits creation. WP-1c(i) flipped the binder allowCrossSchema default from true to false (V1 parity).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the operator must configure both the intervention and profiler.provider=live to get v1-equivalent evidence-gated creation
findings.empiricalCheck: Run v2 publish with intervention registered, profiler off vs live, diff emitted FKs and diagnostics stream (decision.fkNotIntroduced count)

findings.topic: HasDbConstraint on the live extraction path (WP-1a)

v1.behavior: HasFK computed in extraction SQL from #FkReality presence, COALESCE'd to 0; hasDbConstraint flows into the model JSON and the evaluator's DATABASE_CONSTRAINT_PRESENT gate.

v2.behavior: LANDED (2026-07-16): MetadataSnapshotRunner.toBundle sets HasDbConstraint = Option.isSome fkOpt (presence in the #FkReality JOIN via AttrId->FkColumns->FkReality chain); previously hardcoded true so every live reference presented as source-backed and the evidence gate could never see a logical-only reference. Rowset reader threads it through Reference.withConstraintState.

findings.difference: Parity restored on main — the remediation-handoff claim ('WP-1a LANDED') is confirmed in code. Fixed on main 2026-07-16. Pre-fix, the WP-1c gated posture would have been inert on live extractions (everything source-backed).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the source-backed carve-out and logical-only gating are only meaningful when HasDbConstraint is real
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: extract an estate with one logical-only ref (Ignore rule, no physical FK); confirm v2 catalog.snapshot.json carries hasDbConstraint=false for it

findings.topic: #FkReality.DeleteAction consumption (WP-1b — brief asked 'pending?')

v1.behavior: Reflected DeleteAction reaches the evidence path (constraint.OnDeleteAction) but is mangled by the case-sensitive mapper (see fks-v1-evidence-path-cascade-case-bug).

v2.behavior: LANDED (2026-07-16, not pending): ReferenceRow.ReflectedOnDelete carried from the JOIN; OssysRowsetReader resolves OnDelete via chooseOnDeleteAction (reflected representable action outranks the model rule for physically-backed FKs); deleteRuleDivergences emits adapter.ossys.fkReality.deleteActionDivergence when model and reality disagree.

findings.difference: The brief's open question is answered: WP-1b landed 2026-07-16. Residual: an ON DELETE SET DEFAULT reflected action is unrepresentable, silently falls back to the model rule, and is excluded from the divergence diagnostic by design (OssysTranslation.fs:470-475) — a documented-but-unnamed degrade.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — SET DEFAULT FKs are rare hand-added shapes; the CASCADE/SET_NULL mainstream is covered
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: FK deployed CASCADE while model says Protect; v2 emits CASCADE + the divergence warning; v1 emits NO ACTION silently

findings.topic: Orphan evidence collection and shape

v1.behavior: Profiling is a mandatory pipeline stage. ForeignKeyProbeQueryBuilder LEFT-JOIN orphan counts, ForeignKeyOrphanSampleQueryBuilder samples orphan rows, multi-environment consensus (ANY environment orphaned => orphaned) and profile reports; ForeignKeyReality(HasOrphan, OrphanCount, IsNoCheck, ProbeStatus, OrphanSample).

v2.behavior: Profile.ForeignKeyReality (ReferenceKey, HasOrphan, OrphanCount, IsNoCheck, ProbeStatus) populated only when profiler.provider='live' (LiveProfiler.attach enriches Profile.empty; includes orphan samples and FK cardinality distributions). Plus v2-only H-024 analytics: meanChildCount metadata and a suggested-config nudge to re-enable creation when a disabled FK shows active use.

findings.difference: Shape parity on the core reality record. Operationally v1 always profiles; v2's profile is opt-in — combined with the EvidenceMissing inversion this makes the profiler a hard prerequisite for any logical-only FK to materialize under the eject posture. V2 lacks v1's multi-environment consensus; v2 adds cardinality-driven advisory analytics v1 lacks.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — cutover runbooks must wire profiler.provider=live before the eject publish
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane with an orphaned FK: v2 fidelity/diagnostics show tightening.foreignKey.dataHasOrphans with the count; v1 profile snapshot shows OrphanCount + sample

findings.topic: Cross-schema FK gating

v1.behavior: crossSchemaBlocked = crossSchema && !AllowCrossSchema && !hasConstraint (default AllowCrossSchema=false); source-backed cross-schema FKs always emit; blocked logical-only refs get CROSS_SCHEMA rationale.

v2.behavior: Same rule shape inside the intervention (crossesSchema case-insensitive compare; checked only on the clean-profile branch after the source-backed carve-out); WP-1c(i) flipped the binder default to false (was true). WITHOUT an intervention, cross-schema logical-only FKs emit ungated. Golden pins the source-backed cross-schema FK [audit].[ChangeLog]->[dbo].[User].

findings.difference: Parity when the intervention is registered (post-WP-1c(i)); divergent (ungated) under v2's no-intervention default. Fixed on main 2026-07-16 for the binder default.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — OutSystems estates are near-universally single-schema dbo
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture with dbo->audit logical-only ref: v2 gated run withholds with tightening.foreignKey.crossSchemaBlocked; ungated run emits

findings.topic: Cross-catalog FK detection

v1.behavior: Entity.Catalog (db_catalog) compared; crossCatalogBlocked = crossCatalog && !AllowCrossCatalog && !hasConstraint; CROSS_CATALOG rationale; AllowCrossCatalog toggle (default false).

v2.behavior: No detection: ForeignKeyKeepReason.CrossCatalogBlocked is reserved-but-unreachable (no producer); the placebo allowCrossCatalog knob was REMOVED at WP-1d rather than wired. Carrier and emission halves exist: TableId.Catalog carries db_catalog (Coordinates.fs:211-229) and the emitter renders three-part [db].[schema].[table] REFERENCES when the target kind carries a catalog (SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:89-101).

findings.difference: Refines dec-cross-catalog: the knob no longer exists (claim said 'placebo knob' — WP-1d deleted it 2026-07-16 as a fail-closed-config cleanup), the DU variant remains reserved. Practical impact is negligible: both trunks extract one database per run (db_catalog = DB_NAME() constant), so v1's gate is effectively vestigial too; truly-external targets fail v2 catalog construction (danglingTarget) rather than reaching an FK gate.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — only estates with linked-server/external-catalog entities, which OutSystems models as external entities anyway
findings.empiricalCheck: Construct a catalog with a kind carrying Physical.Catalog=Some other and a reference to it; observe the three-part REFERENCES render and no gate

findings.topic: Derived inverse references never become FKs

v1.behavior: No synthetic inverses exist — relationships are directional model facts; pure-target entities emit zero FKs by construction.

v2.behavior: SymmetricClosure synthesizes inverse references with DerivedFrom(_, Inverse) SsKeys; Reference.isDeployable excludes them from FK tightening (pass v3) and every constraint-emission surface; golden negative invariant holds (dbo.User emits zero FKs).

findings.difference: Parity restored (WP1 of the reconciliation plan): the pre-reconciliation duplicate-FK-on-target bug is fixed and pinned as a golden negative invariant. BUT the inverses remain in the topological-order graph — see fks-topo-order-defeated for the consequence.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — CreatedBy/UpdatedBy shapes were the original defect class
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: publish and grep pure-target kind files for FOREIGN KEY (expect none)

findings.topic: Topological ordering of DDL stream and data lanes is structurally defeated by symmetric-closure inverses

v1.behavior: EntityDependencySorter builds edges from real FK relationships only (no synthetic inverses), resolves cycles (Tarjan + weak-edge classification + manual positions + feedback-arc search) and falls back alphabetically ONLY for residual cycle members, preserving partial topological order; used for static seeds and bootstrap data ordering. DDL ordering is delegated to DacFx.

v2.behavior: The compose chain runs SymmetricClosure (step 5) BEFORE TopologicalOrderPass (step 9), and buildGraphWithin iterates k.References WITHOUT the isDeployable filter — every FK pair becomes a 2-cycle whose inverse edge classifies strong (source attr not found or non-nullable => Other), so weakFeedbackStrategy refuses and the WHOLE catalog degrades to Mode=Alphabetical (SsKey order). EMPIRICALLY REPRODUCED byte-identically: pre-chain GoldenCatalog => Mode=Topological/6 edges; post-chain => Mode=Alphabetical/10 edges; regenerated stream.sql equals the pinned golden exactly. The pinned stream creates [audit].[ChangeLog] (line 69, inline FK REFERENCES [dbo].[User]) 1057 lines BEFORE CREATE TABLE [dbo].[User] (line 1126) — sequential execution fails (Msg 1767). Data lanes walk the same degraded order (DataEmissionComposer global phase-1); nullable FK columns get blanket two-phase deferral (every FK-connected kind is a 'cycle member'), non-nullable FKs ride phase-1 in SsKey/GUID order.

findings.difference: Refutes dec-cross-module-fk's ordering half ('TopologicalOrderPass ... so targets emit before referencers'): on the production publish chain the pass NEVER orders any FK-bearing catalog — the golden corpus byte-pins the alphabetical fallback. Blast radius: (a) stream.sql is not sequentially deployable against an empty database (any Render.toText-based deploy path); (b) data-lane Bootstrap/MigrationData phase-1 order is arbitrary (SsKey/GUID), so any NOT NULL FK whose parent sorts later fails at load against enforced FKs; (c) estate-wide over-deferral of every nullable FK column inflates phase-2 UPDATE volume/CDC. The per-table DacFx bundle is unaffected (DacFx computes its own order). Transfer-lane paths read non-closure catalogs and are unaffected.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every real estate has non-nullable FKs (mandatory refs), so every full-chain publish degrades; bootstrap loads of mandatory-FK children precede their parents
findings.empiricalCheck: Deploy Golden/master/stream.sql batch-by-batch to an empty DB on localhost:11433 and observe Msg 1767 at [audit].[ChangeLog]; or run a live publish and grep manifest/diagnostics for 'alphabetical fallback'

findings.topic: Cycle resolution machinery (classifier + resolver + operator levers)

v1.behavior: Classifier: Weak = nullable + NO_ACTION/SET_NULL; Cascade = CASCADE (nullable or not); Other = rest (CascadeWeak enum member exists but is dead — ClassifyRelationship never returns it). Resolver arsenal: auto asymmetric-2-cycle, multi-node weak-edge minimum-feedback-arc search (50k combination cap), name heuristics (HISTORY/AUDIT/LOG/ARCHIVE/VERSION scoring), manual AllowedCycle z-index positions (disables auto-detection when configured), StrictMode fail-on-any-cycle, alphabetical fallback for residual members only.

v2.behavior: Identical classifier semantics (classify: Cascade never breakable even when nullable — 'conservative choice kept from V1'; Weak = nullable + NoAction/SetNull; parallel-edge strengths combine, breakable only if ALL parallel refs weak). Resolver v5 weakFeedbackStrategy: deterministic greedy break-smallest-weak-edge-per-residual-cycle with proved invariants (soundness/acyclicity/refusal-precision/determinism/frugality); refusal names the exact cycle; whole-catalog alphabetical on refusal. Manual overrides deliberately NOT carried (deferred with named trigger, DECISIONS 2026-07-07 — espace-fragile physical-name keying rejected); config allowedCycles is annotate-only (WP-13); no StrictMode, no name heuristics.

findings.difference: Classifier parity (v1's CascadeWeak is dead code). Resolver: v2 is more principled and deterministic but removes every operator lever v1 had (manual positions, strict mode); refusal degrades the ENTIRE order rather than v1's partial-topological + alphabetical-appendix. Confirms the task brief's 'v2 automatic weak-edge + allowedCycles annotate-only'. Note the resolver's quality is currently moot on the publish chain (see fks-topo-order-defeated).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — matters for estates with genuine strong FK cycles needing operator-staged loads
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture with an all-strong 3-cycle: v2 refuses naming members and orders alphabetically; v1 with AllowedCycle positions orders per config

findings.topic: Junction-table deferral

v1.behavior: --defer-junction-tables CLI option, DEFAULT TRUE, wired into static-seed and bootstrap ordering; JunctionTableClassifier requires relationships with actual constraints, >=2 distinct non-key columns, FK-column coverage analysis.

v2.behavior: H-040 JunctionDeferred mode exists at the pass level (isJunctionKind: >=2 FK references AND <=2 non-PK attributes; runWithConfig/OrderingConfig.DeferJunctionKinds) but no Pipeline/Config wiring found — the production chain registers the default TreatAsCycle pass; junction deferral is config-unreachable.

findings.difference: V1's data lanes defer junctions by default; v2 has the mechanism but no operator path to it, and its junction predicate differs from v1's (attribute-count based vs column/FK-coverage based) so classifications will diverge at edges when it is wired.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — affects seed/bootstrap ordering aesthetics and rarely correctness (junctions have no dependents)
findings.empiricalCheck: grep v2 Pipeline/Config for DeferJunctionKinds consumers (none); compare v1 seed ordering with --defer-junction-tables true/false

findings.topic: Two-phase (NULL-then-UPDATE) cyclic data loading

v1.behavior: PhasedDynamicEntityInsertGenerator: phase-1 MERGE with nullable FKs NULLed for cycle tables, phase-2 UPDATE re-points them (on the deprecated dynamic-insert path); seeds/bootstrap rely on ordering.

v2.behavior: MergeRender phase-1 MERGE excludes deferred columns from insert and WHEN-MATCHED update; phase-2 UPDATEs re-point; DataEmissionComposer orders ALL phase-1 across kinds before ANY phase-2 (slice-iota global ordering); deferredFkColumns = nullable FK columns of cycle members; non-nullable cycle FKs surface as transfer.unbreakableCycleFk refusals on the transfer lane. Golden pins RegionA/B: phase-1 VALUES(...,NULL), trailing UPDATE SET PartnerId=1.

findings.difference: Mechanism parity with a v2 refinement (global phase ordering; named unbreakable refusal vs v1's silent best-effort). V2's blanket cycle-membership (from the inverse-edge defeat) currently over-applies the deferral estate-wide.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — static-entity cycles (region/partner shapes) exist in real estates
findings.empiricalCheck: Load golden StaticSeeds.sql on warm SQL with FKs present; verify both phases apply and final PartnerId values are 1

findings.topic: FK trust re-establishment after load/remediation

v1.behavior: Advice only: remediation scripts and opportunity logs append commented guidance '-- After remediation run: ALTER TABLE .. WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT ..'; nothing executable.

v2.behavior: Executable: TransferFkTrust snapshots pre-load trust, NOCHECKs FKs on loaded tables, re-validates with WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT post-load for previously-trusted FKs; EstateRemediation emits WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT ALL re-trust scripts; ReadSide reads sys.foreign_keys.is_not_trusted and PhysicalSchema.diff round-trips the trust decision (M1 retired the FkTrustUnreflected tolerance).

findings.difference: V2 automates what v1 only documented, and proves trust state round-trips through the readback comparator. V2-only capability class for the cutover transfer lane.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — bulk transfer loads must not leave every FK untrusted
findings.empiricalCheck: Docker transfer-lane canary: run a transfer and query is_not_trusted before/after (CanaryRoundTripTests cover the readback leg)

findings.topic: FK name collision handling

v1.behavior: processedConstraints HashSet keyed viaAttribute|name|ownerCols|refCols dedups duplicate evidence rows (first wins, silently); it does NOT catch two different FKs sharing a schema-scoped name — both emit and the failure surfaces only at DacFx build.

v2.behavior: Loud Error tripwire: emitted FKs grouped by (schema, name); any group >1 produces one emit.ssdt.foreignKey.nameCollision Error per participating reference; structurally unreachable for forward references (name embeds source column) — the tripwire guards the invariant. Plus WP-16 added the CREATE TABLE mirror (emit.ssdt.table.nameCollision).

findings.difference: Claim pkt-e5's mechanics refined: v1's HashSet was a duplicate-evidence dedup (its key includes columns), not a general name-collision mask — but it did swallow the inverse-emission defect class's duplicate rows; v2 names collisions as Errors before deployment. Confirmed v2-better with the mechanics corrected.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — reachable mainly via renames/overrides colliding synthesized names
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture with two same-named source columns on renamed tables producing colliding FK names; expect the Error diagnostic pre-deploy

findings.topic: Deployed FK constraint names

v1.behavior: Evidence path honors the provided constraint name (match.ProvidedConstraintName -> ForeignKeyNameFactory.CreateEvidenceName), falling back to synthesis; 128-char hash-truncation via ConstraintNameNormalizer.

v2.behavior: Always synthesizes FK_<OwnerTable>_<TargetTable>_<SourceColumn> with IdentifierBudget.fit 128-cap; Reference.Name is carried in IR but NEVER read by fkDef — the open WP7 remainder, explicitly noted in code.

findings.difference: For a source-backed FK whose deployed name differs from the synthesis convention (common: OutSystems platform FK names embed physical OSUSR names and hashes), v2's emitted project renames every such constraint — first schema-compare/deploy against the live estate drops and recreates each FK under the new name (churn + breaks any external scripts referencing constraint names). V1 preserved deployed names on the evidence path.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — every platform-created FK on a real estate carries a platform name that will not match the synthesis
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: extract an estate table with platform-named FK, publish, sqlpackage script against the source DB and count FK drop/create pairs

findings.topic: FK to composite-PK target

v1.behavior: Evidence path supports multi-column constraints (ordered constraint.Columns resolved to owner+referenced column lists) — composite FKs re-emit faithfully; fallback path is single-column to the preferred identifier.

v2.behavior: Reference IR is single-column (SourceAttribute: one SsKey); fkDef references the target's FIRST IsPrimaryKey attribute; PhysicalSchema.toPhysicalForeignKeys pairs only the first PK leg and names the gap via ToleratedDivergence.CompositePkFkUnreflected (NM-28) instead of silently collapsing; guard is the operator-confirmed 'OutSystems never does this'.

findings.difference: Confirmed capability gap: a composite FK (hand-added or external) emits only its first leg — invalid SQL unless that column carries its own unique index; tolerance-tracked (OpenGap) rather than silent. V1 handled it via evidence.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — impossible from the platform model; possible on estate databases with hand-added composite FKs
findings.empiricalCheck: ReadSide-derived catalog from a DB with a composite FK; publish and attempt DacFx build (expect failure or wrong single-leg FK)

findings.topic: FK whose target is absent from the export

v1.behavior: Evaluator silently returns CreateConstraint per remaining gates with no opportunity record for the missing-target case; if a decision approves and the target is unresolvable at SMO build, the export CRASHES with InvalidOperationException ('Target entity ... not found').

v2.behavior: Three-layer handling: Catalog.create REJECTS dangling targets at ingest (catalog.reference.danglingTarget); ForeignKeyRules names MissingTarget as the first-checked keep-reason (outranks all gates incl. source-backed); emitter defense-in-depth witnesses emit.ssdt.foreignKey.unresolvedTargetDropped / targetMissingPrimaryKeyDropped (the PK-less-target case is the genuinely reachable one).

findings.difference: Confirms adm-fk-missing-target and extends it: v1's failure mode is either invisibility (evaluator) or a hard crash (SMO); v2 is fail-loud at ingest with named refusals downstream. Note the practical corollary: a module-scoped v2 export whose references leave the scope fails catalog construction outright — operators must export closure-complete sets.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — module-filtered exports routinely reference out-of-scope system entities
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture missing a referenced kind: v2 ingest errors catalog.reference.danglingTarget; v1 either emits nothing for it or throws at build-ssdt

findings.topic: Decision algebra shape (two-boolean vs binary outcome + evidence)

v1.behavior: ForeignKeyDecision(CreateConstraint, ScriptWithNoCheck) + sorted rationale strings (DATABASE_CONSTRAINT_PRESENT, DATA_HAS_ORPHANS, DELETE_RULE_IGNORE, POLICY_ENABLE_CREATION, ...); DELETE_RULE_IGNORE rationale attaches even when the constraint IS created (rationale-on-success); Cautious-mode gate on the NOCHECK branch.

v2.behavior: ForeignKeyOutcome = EnforceConstraint of ForeignKeyEvidence | DoNotEnforce of ForeignKeyKeepReason (typed DUs; ScriptWithNoCheck folded into evidence with orphanCount); Cautious mode collapsed into AllowNoCheckCreation; enforced-with-Ignore emits no diagnostic (DeleteRuleIgnored unreachable — isIgnoreRule hardcoded false pending WP-1c posture); config now 3 of v1's 5 toggles (WP-1d removed 2) + Overrides + Direction.

findings.difference: Confirms adm-fk-evaluator with two refinements: (a) 'five toggles carried verbatim' is stale — WP-1d deleted AllowCrossCatalog and TreatMissingDeleteRuleAsIgnore, and the estate A6 amendment added Overrides/Direction; (b) the DeleteRuleIgnore rationale-on-success divergence is confirmed in current code (v1 always tags it; v2 never does).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — audit-log consumers comparing v1 rationale strings to v2 DUs need the mapping
findings.empiricalCheck: Compare v1 policy-decision report rationales vs v2 decision-log entries for the same fixture

findings.topic: Cross-module FK target resolution at the adapter

v1.behavior: Model JSON carries no refEntity_module; entity lookup resolves reference targets across the whole model via EntityEmissionIndex (logical/physical name), module-agnostic.

v2.behavior: Rowset/live path is cross-module-correct: primary resolution via global RefEntityId map, fallback by entity name across EVERY module, same-module synthesis only as last resort — the file's own comment at lines 170-172 still claims 'rule 16 same-module assumption' and is STALE against the code directly below it. JSON path retains the true same-module synthesis (kindSsKey sourceModuleName refEntityName).

findings.difference: Refines dec-cross-module-fk: the IR-level cross-module distinction is real and solved on the live path; only the JSON path keeps the same-module assumption (a cross-module ref there mints a dangling target key that Catalog.create rejects loudly). The claim's 'ordering handles it' half is refuted separately (fks-topo-order-defeated).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — cross-module references are ubiquitous (User, shared cores); live path is the cutover path
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture-lane model JSON with a cross-module ref: expect catalog.reference.danglingTarget on the JSON path, clean resolution on the rowset path

findings.topic: FK file placement (inline vs post-deploy)

v1.behavior: Single-column FKs inline beneath their column; multi-column at table level; all in the table's object file (NOCHECK ones as trailing ALTERs in the same file).

v2.behavior: Same inline pattern (attachInlineForeignKey mirrors V1 CreateTableStatementBuilder.cs:197-202 per the operator blessing); PostDeployForeignKeysSplit is a named tolerance reserving the sanctioned fallback of splitting cross-module FKs into a post-deploy artifact if deploy-order failures surface — not exercised today.

findings.difference: Parity on layout; the tolerance's own trigger condition ('deploy-order failures surface') is arguably already met by the stream-order defeat (fks-topo-order-defeated) for stream-based deploys, though DacFx bundle deploys — the primary path — do not need the split.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes
findings.empiricalCheck: Compare deployed FK sets (sys.foreign_keys) after v1 and v2 bundle deploys, not file layout

findings.topic: Multiple-cascade-path (msg 1785) pre-analysis

v1.behavior: None — no reference to 1785/cascade-path analysis anywhere; Delete->CASCADE maps verbatim and DacFx/SQL Server surfaces Msg 1785 at deploy.
v1.cite: src/Osm.Smo/SmoEntityEmitter.cs:179-180 (verbatim map; repo-wide grep for 1785 empty)

v2.behavior: Same verbatim map today; WP-1e (the msg-1785 cascade-path pre-analyzer) is named backlog; v2 additionally ships the cascade-shock-zone advisory (topology.cascadeShock Warning for >=3 kinds reachable via CASCADE edges) — adjacent risk surfacing, not 1785 detection.
v2.cite: sidecar/projection/DECISIONS.md WP-1c(ii) scope note ('the only WP-1 remainder is WP-1e') + Projection.Core/Passes/TopologicalOrderPass.fs:761-877

findings.topic: Referenced column of emitted FKs

v1.behavior: Evidence path emits the constraint's actual referenced columns (which may be a non-PK unique column); fallback references the preferred identifier.

v2.behavior: fkDef always references the target kind's first IsPrimaryKey attribute; there is no channel for a non-PK referenced column in the Reference IR.

findings.difference: A deployed FK referencing a non-PK unique column would be silently re-pointed at the PK by v2 (changed relationship semantics), where v1's evidence path preserved it. No tolerance token names this case (unlike composite-PK).

findings.topic: Reference IR conflation costs (rows 57/58/59)

v1.behavior: Three-type split: RelationshipModel (logical) / ForeignKeyModel (physical, with UpdateRule) / RelationshipActualConstraint (reconciliation, multi-column, named).

v2.behavior: Single Reference record; OnUpdate (row 58) closed via parseSqlForeignKeyAction; per-constraint trust (row 59) closed via ConstraintState DU (M4 collapsed the HasDbConstraint/IsConstraintTrusted boolean pair into NoDbConstraint|TrustedConstraint|UntrustedConstraint with the illegal quadrant unrepresentable) driving the NOCHECK two-step; FK NAMES (row 57/WP7) remain un-honored.

findings.difference: Claim adm-reference-conflation confirmed and updated: the ConstraintState DU (chapter 4.6/M4) post-dates the claim and strengthens the trust carriage; the WP7 name remainder is the only open cost (see fks-constraint-names-not-honored).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes
findings.empiricalCheck: Codec round-trip: serialize/deserialize a catalog with an untrusted FK; ReferenceConstraintStateTests witness the legacy-boolean wire form

findings.topic: G5(a): manifest.remediation.sql at bundle root is not Build-Removed from the SDK sqlproj glob (claim pkt-g5-wired-hazards, hazard a) — CONFIRMED still live at ea21b13

v1.behavior: Remediation artifacts live in a subdirectory (suggestions/safe-to-apply.sql + suggestions/needs-remediation.sql) and the v1 sqlproj explicitly enumerates only Modules/**/*.sql (Build) and Seeds/**/*.sql (PostDeploy) from disk, so stray root .sql can never enter the build.

v2.behavior: manifest.remediation.sql is written unconditionally at bundle root on every publish; SqlprojEmitter Build-Removes only Script.PostDeployment.sql and the Data/*.sql lanes, so the Microsoft.Build.Sql default **/*.sql glob compiles the remediation file. With findings present it carries active SELECT statements ('OPTION 1 (SELECT) is active'), which are not valid schema objects — dotnet build of the emitted project breaks. The gated build test hand-assembles the project dir without the remediation file, so the witness never fires.

findings.difference: V2's move from explicit item enumeration (v1) to SDK default globbing leaves one unconditional root .sql artifact inside the Build glob. Fires exactly under emission.sqlproj:true + any remediation finding — the recommended eject-handoff configuration. Packet §8 item 7 tracks it with no WP tag; no fix landed in #669/#670/#671.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — a real estate with live profiling almost always has ≥1 nullability/orphan/duplicate finding, so the handed-off project fails dotnet build
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: full-export with emission.sqlproj:true against a fixture (or live localhost:11433) seeded with one FK orphan; add a nuget.config to the output dir and run dotnet build ProjectionCatalog.sqlproj — expect a build error naming manifest.remediation.sql; delete the file and rebuild clean.

findings.topic: G5(b): wired post-deploy :r order is alphabetical — MigrationData before StaticSeeds — against the emitter's pinned deploy order (claim pkt-g5-wired-hazards, hazard b) — CONFIRMED still live

v1.behavior: No migration lane exists in v1 (concept absent); v1's post-deploy template orders the guarded Bootstrap include first, then baseline seeds, and its FK preflight (StaticSeedForeignKeyPreflight) warns on seed-order risks.

v2.behavior: The wired sqlproj path builds the lane list via Map.toList |> List.sort then filters Bootstrap out, yielding :r Data/MigrationData.sql before :r Data/StaticSeeds.sql. The emitter's own unit test pins StaticSeeds BEFORE MigrationData, and the Docker deploy E2E passes StaticSeeds first — both witnesses exercise a hand-ordered list, never the wired sort.

findings.difference: The wired :r include order inverts the intended (test-pinned) deploy order. If any operator-curated migration-dependency row FKs into a static-entity row (e.g. a User row referencing a static Role), sqlpackage publish fails the post-deploy on FK violation. Each lane is internally topo-ordered; only the cross-lane order is wrong.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — fires only with emission.sqlproj:true + both lanes non-empty + a migration-kind→static-kind FK; migrationDependencies files curating User-like kinds commonly reference statics
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane vs localhost:11433: seed a static kind + a migration kind FK'ing it, run publish with sqlproj:true + a migrationDependencies file; assert Script.PostDeployment.sql line index of MigrationData < StaticSeeds; then dotnet build + DacServices.Deploy and observe the INSERT/MERGE FK failure; reorder and observe success.

findings.topic: G3: accumulated refactorlog now reaches the bundle + .sqlproj (claim pkt-g3-refactorlog-gap) — FIXED on main 2026-07-16 (#671); v1 comparison inverted: V1 never emitted any refactorlog

v1.behavior: Zero refactorlog capability anywhere: grep for refactorlog/RefactorLog over src/, tests/, tools/, docs/ returns nothing; the legacy sqlproj emitter has no RefactorLog item. Incremental deploys of v1 output always DROP+CREATE on rename.

v2.behavior: Store-threaded publish writes ProjectionCatalog.refactorlog inside the atomic bundle (Outputs.RefactorLog → staging writer), computed as prior chain ⊕ this run's displacement deduped by OperationKey, rendered in DEPLOYED vocabulary via RefactorLogEmitter.emitDeployed with operator-renamed kinds suppressed; emission.sqlproj:true adds the explicit <RefactorLog> item; DacFx canary proves sp_rename end-to-end (rows survive rename with BlockOnPossibleDataLoss armed). Presence contract: file ⟺ store-threaded; store-less runs are byte-identical genesis.

findings.difference: Fixed on main 2026-07-16. V2 now strictly exceeds v1 on the rename channel. Standing residuals an operator must know: (1) a publish WITHOUT --lifecycle-store/env store still ships no refactorlog — renames DROP+CREATE (by design; the store is the rename evidence); (2) operator tableRenames rewrite Kind.Physical only and are invisible to the Name-keyed rename channel; (3) FK-rename channel named-absent until WP-7; (4) file-sourced models cannot thread rename identity (6.A.7).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — Service-Studio entity renames between releases are routine; incremental publish semantics (sp_rename vs DROP+CREATE) decide whether data survives
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane: full-export A --lifecycle-store s.json → dotnet build + Deploy to localhost:11433 → seed rows → rename entity in model → full-export B with same store → verify ProjectionCatalog.refactorlog exists + .sqlproj carries the RefactorLog item → incremental publish → rows survive under new name. Then repeat WITHOUT the store and confirm no .refactorlog is written.

findings.topic: G6: CREATE SCHEMA objects emitted as Schemas/<name>.sql (claim pkt-g6-no-create-schema) — FIXED on main 2026-07-16 (#671); v1 never emitted CREATE SCHEMA either

v1.behavior: No CREATE SCHEMA emission anywhere (grep over src/ empty); a non-dbo v1 estate also required hand-authored schema objects before its output could deploy fresh.
v1.cite: src/Osm.Emission + src/Osm.Smo (whole-tree grep for 'CREATE SCHEMA'/'CreateSchema' empty)

v2.behavior: SsdtDdlEmitter.nonDboSchemas derives every distinct non-dbo schema (kinds + sequences, dbo excluded any-case, CI-deduped); one Schemas/<name>.sql per schema joins the bundle via SsdtBundle.composeWithSchemas (SDK default Build glob compiles them); CREATE SCHEMA opens the statement stream; the dacpac arm admits CreateSchema as a declarative model member. Golden master pins Schemas/audit.sql and the stream head. Note: no IF NOT EXISTS guard — correct for DacFx diffing, but raw re-execution of the stream against a target with a pre-created schema refuses loudly.
v2.cite: sidecar/projection/src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:607-631; SsdtBundle.fs:49-62; DacpacEmitter.fs:83-85; tests/Projection.Tests/Golden/master/Schemas/audit.sql + stream.sql:1-4; DECISIONS.md 2026-07-16 G6 entry

findings.difference: Fixed on main 2026-07-16, and v2 now closes a gap that was SHARED with v1 (the packet framed G6 as a v2 gap; v1 had the identical hole). dbo-only estates byte-identical.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — most OutSystems estates are dbo-only; bites exactly when external/DBA schemas (audit-style) exist
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: emit a catalog with an audit-schema kind; assert Schemas/audit.sql present and stream head is CREATE SCHEMA; dotnet build the sqlproj to a .dacpac (pre-G6 refuses SQL71501); dbo-only fixture emits no Schemas/ dir.

findings.topic: F8: post-deploy carries StaticSeeds+MigrationData only; Bootstrap is a separate manual post-publish step (claim pkt-f8-bootstrap-placement) — CONFIRMED incl. the DECISIONS doc gap; v1's template auto-included a guarded bootstrap

v1.behavior: PostDeployment-Bootstrap.sql template (output root) :r-includes Bootstrap\AllEntitiesIncludingStatic.bootstrap.sql wrapped in an IF NOT EXISTS first-deployment guard, then :r-includes every baseline seed file (backslash paths); the sqlproj separately marks all Seeds/**/*.sql as PostDeploy items. Seeds default layout is per-module (GroupByModule=true): Seeds/<Module>/StaticEntities.seed.sql.

v2.behavior: Script.PostDeployment.sql :r-includes only the StaticSeeds + MigrationData lanes; Data/Bootstrap.sql is Build-Removed as a None item and NEVER :r-included — no verb executes it; the operator runs it via sqlcmd post-publish (runbook step 6a) or uses the --load leveled path. The 2026-06-24 operator decision exists only in the PostDeployEmitter docstring — DECISIONS.md has zero 2026-06-24 entries (grep confirmed). V1's BaselineSeeds/per-module grouping is left behind for one whole-estate Data/StaticSeeds.sql.

findings.difference: Claim confirmed in full. The operator-visible delta: v1's first-deploy bootstrap load was automated inside the publish (guarded include); v2 makes it an explicit manual step the receiving pipeline must add — a missed step leaves every non-static table empty after cutover with a green publish. The governing decision remains undocumented in DECISIONS.md (doc gap confirmed by the packet's own §8 drift list).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the bulk data load is the cutover's center of mass; the choreography difference lands directly on the receiving team's runbook
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: publish with sqlproj:true + data emission on; assert Script.PostDeployment.sql contains no Bootstrap :r and the .sqlproj None's Data/Bootstrap.sql; build+publish to localhost:11433 and verify non-static tables are empty until Data/Bootstrap.sql is run via sqlcmd.

findings.topic: manifest.json schema: v1 SsdtManifest vs v2 Manifest (claim dec-manifest-scope) — CONFIRMED with refinements: drop set as claimed, but v2 has since grown far past '6 fields' and per-table entries changed payload

v1.behavior: 8 top-level PascalCase fields (default System.Text.Json naming): Tables, Options{IncludePlatformAutoIndexes,EmitBareTableOnly,SanitizeModuleNames,ModuleParallelism}, PolicySummary (13-field decision rollup incl. rationale counts + toggle precedence), Emission{Algorithm:'SHA256',Hash} model+decisions fingerprint, PreRemediation (per-table hash entries), Coverage, PredicateCoverage (PredicateCounts as JSON dict), Unsupported. TableManifestEntry carries Indexes/ForeignKeys as NAME LISTS + IncludesExtendedProperties.

v2.behavior: camelCase keys: emitter, version, registry{digest}, tables, coverage, predicateCoverage (predicateCounts as sorted {name,count} ARRAY), unsupported, preRemediation (always []), columnProfiles, deploymentBatches, plus conditional policy/policyConflicts/appliedTransforms/centrality/boundedContexts/profileAnomalies/schemaComplexity/queryHints/cascadeShockZones. Options + PolicySummary dropped per A18; no model-content hash (RegistryDigest is a transform-registry digest; content identity moved to catalog.snapshot.json + Run.inputDigest). TableManifestEntry carries IndexCount/ForeignKeyCount INTS only.

findings.difference: Claim's drop/add set confirmed (Options+PolicySummary out; EmitterVersion+RegistryDigest in; PreRemediation deferred; predicateCounts shape diverges) but stale on size — v2's manifest now carries ~10 always-on sections plus analytics. Consumer-breaking deltas: key casing (PascalCase→camelCase), per-table names→counts (a consumer diffing FK/index NAME sets per table loses that from the manifest), IncludesExtendedProperties gone, no content hash.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — bites exactly if the estate's CI/audit tooling parses v1's manifest.json (V2's own docstring says 'V1's existing pipeline expects it')
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: run v1 full-export and v2 publish on the same model; jq -S keys of both manifest.json files and diff; assert v2 tables[0] has indexCount/foreignKeyCount ints where v1 has Indexes/ForeignKeys arrays.

findings.topic: Same-named manifest fields with silently different semantics: Unsupported and Coverage

v1.behavior: Unsupported = per-run coverage-shortfall detector output — free-text messages like 'Primary key skipped for X: no emittable identifier columns' / 'Table X missing from emission output', computed by comparing the model against the actually-built SMO model; Coverage = real emitted-vs-expected counts that can be <100%.

v2.behavior: unsupported = the STATIC closed ToleratedDivergence vocabulary — Unsupported.compute () takes unit and renders the same sorted list every run regardless of catalog; the per-run FIRED subset lives in fidelity.json/. txt as ACCEPTED DIVERGENCES via Tolerance.matchedResidual (configured ∩ observed). coverage = CoverageSummary.createComplete — Emitted=Total by construction (T11 keyset), always 100%.

findings.difference: A consumer reading v2's 'unsupported' as v1's per-run defect list (or v2's always-100% coverage as an independent emission check) is misled; v2's real per-run divergence account is fidelity.json/.txt and its real independent check is the round-trip canary, not the manifest. This also refines claim pkt-manifest-tolerance-inband (confirmed: manifest publishes the vocabulary; fidelity publishes the fired residual).
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — same trigger as the manifest-schema finding; operators eyeballing manifest.json during cutover verification will read vacuous coverage
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: two v2 runs on wildly different catalogs → identical 'unsupported' arrays and 100% coverage in both manifests; a live run with a tolerance fired → fidelity.txt ACCEPTED DIVERGENCES section lists exactly the fired subset.

findings.topic: Per-run operator artifact set: v1's decision-log/opportunity/telemetry/run-manifest files have no v2 bundle analogs (claims pkt-g1 + pkt-scope-fidelity-artifacts, enriched)

v1.behavior: Output tree includes policy-decisions.json + policy-decision-report.json, opportunities.json + validations.json, suggestions/safe-to-apply.sql + suggestions/needs-remediation.sql (safe-vs-remediation split with an optional auto-apply leg), pipeline-telemetry.zip (manifest+decision log+both suggestion scripts), profile.json, DynamicData/ (deprecated), and full-export.manifest.json — a machine-readable run-artifact inventory consumed by verify-data --manifest.

v2.behavior: Unconditional artifacts: Modules/** + Schemas/* + manifest.json, projection.json, distributions.json, manifest.remediation.sql (single file, SELECT active + destructive commented, no auto-apply), manifest.summary.txt, suggest-config.json, fidelity.json/.txt, catalog.snapshot.json; opt-in Data/*, projection.dacpac, ProjectionCatalog.sqlproj + Script.PostDeployment.sql, ProjectionCatalog.refactorlog. No per-column decision-log JSON, no opportunities/validations files, no telemetry zip, no run-artifact-inventory file in the bundle — run provenance rides the NDJSON LogSink stream + the episode store instead.

findings.difference: The bundle's ancillary contract is re-shaped, not shrunk: v2 adds fidelity/catalog-snapshot/suggest-config, drops v1's decision-log JSONs, the safe/remediation script split, the auto-apply path, the telemetry package, and the run manifest. Teams with audit or CI steps keyed on v1 filenames (safe-to-apply.sql, policy-decisions.json, full-export.manifest.json) must rebuild those steps on NDJSON + fidelity.json.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — pure workflow surface; bites any operator automation written against v1's artifact names
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: run both CLIs on the same model into fresh dirs and diff the file trees (find . -type f | sort); confirm none of v1's five decision/opportunity filenames exist in the v2 tree.

findings.topic: sqlproj generation: v1 always-on legacy skeleton vs v2 opt-in SDK project (claim pkt-g2-sqlproj) — CONFIRMED with two refinements: v1's artifact is not buildable, and #671 stales the 'no RefactorLog item' clause

v1.behavior: Always writes OutSystemsModel.sqlproj (legacy msbuild-2003 format: DSP Sql160DatabaseSchemaProvider, ModelCollation 1033,CI, DefaultCollation SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2, ProjectVersion 4.2) with explicit Build items per module file and one PostDeploy item PER seed file — but NO <Import> of the SSDT targets, so it cannot build anywhere; and with default per-module seeds it declares multiple PostDeploy items, which classic SSDT rejects (one post-deploy per project). It is an inventory artifact, not a project.

v2.behavior: Opt-in (emission.sqlproj default false) SDK-style Microsoft.Build.Sql/2.2.0 project: Name/DSP Sql160/ModelCollation '1033, CI' only; relies on the SDK's default Build glob; Build-Removes + reclassifies post-deploy (PostDeploy) and data lanes (None); since #671 conditionally carries the <RefactorLog> item; proven dotnet-buildable to a .dacpac by the gated test (incl. the non-dbo G6 case). Still not emitted: nuget.config (restore requires one — SS11 gap 6), SqlCmd vars, publish profiles, DacVersion, PreDeploy. The sqlproj feature itself still has no DECISIONS.md entry (grep: only the G3 entry mentions it).

findings.difference: Claim confirmed and sharpened: the v1 'sqlproj step' the parity ledger compares against was never a working deploy path (missing targets import + multi-PostDeploy), while v2's opt-in project is the actual DacFx package path — so the operator-facing delta is v2 gaining the real capability, not losing v1 parity. Stale clause in the mined claim/packet G2 register: 'no RefactorLog item' is superseded by #671 (conditionally present). Doc gaps (no DECISIONS entry for the feature; bundle ships no nuget.config) both confirmed.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — the sqlproj build is the sanctioned route from bundle to target (runbook step 5)
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: attempt msbuild of v1's OutSystemsModel.sqlproj (fails: no targets); dotnet build v2's ProjectionCatalog.sqlproj with a hand-added nuget.config (succeeds, .dacpac produced); confirm restore fails without nuget.config.

findings.topic: Output-directory semantics: v1 merge-in-place vs v2 atomic replace (claim pkt-g1-bundle-layout, replace clause) — CONFIRMED

v1.behavior: SsdtEmitter CreateDirectory + per-file writes into the existing output dir; nothing deletes prior content, so a table removed from the model leaves its stale .sql behind — and because the sqlproj step re-enumerates *.sql FROM DISK, stale files from prior runs are pulled into the project's Build items.

v2.behavior: writeWith stages every artifact into a sibling .staging-<guid> dir, then Directory.Delete(outputDir) + Directory.Move(staging) — atomic on one volume; failure leaves the prior dir byte-identical; success REPLACES (never merges), with a documented operator caveat that outputDir is V2-owned and a named non-atomic crash window between delete and move.

findings.difference: V2 structurally prevents stale-artifact drift (the v1 failure mode where removed entities linger in both the tree and the project), at the cost of destroying any operator-placed files in outputDir and a tiny crash window leaving the dir absent.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — repeated per-release exports into the same directory are the steady-state workflow; v1's stale files become phantom tables at deploy
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: run v1 twice with an entity removed between runs — stale .sql remains and appears in the sqlproj; run v2 twice the same way — the file is gone; kill -9 v2 mid-write and confirm the prior output dir is untouched.

findings.topic: Dacpac: v2 opt-in DacFx package; v1 has none (claim pkt-g4-dacpac) — CONFIRMED with one wording refinement

v1.behavior: Zero dacpac/DacFx capability (whole-src grep for dacpac/DacFx/DacServices: empty); the only package-ish artifact is the non-buildable sqlproj skeleton.

v2.behavior: emission.dacpac (default false) compiles the emitted catalog to .dacpac bytes in-process over the same bound emission seam as the bundle; schema-only by construction (DacFx 162.5.57 PackageMetadata exposes no PostDeploymentScript — post-deploy embeds only via the sqlproj build); T1 amended to content-equality (Origin.xml wall-clock defeats byte-diff); NM-24: the package is REFUSED, with each object named, when any schema statement fails to render (e.g. unparseable trigger body) — refinement: the refusal is on UNRENDERED statements, not 'objects that would be dropped at deploy'. Written as projection.dacpac inside the atomic bundle.

findings.difference: Pure v2 capability addition (dev-tooling; the sqlproj build stays the production package path). Golden corpus deliberately excludes it.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — useful for dev one-click stand-up; not on the production deploy path
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: publish twice with emission.dacpac:true; byte-diff the two projection.dacpac (differ), then DacPackage.Load both and compare model object sets (equal); feed a catalog with an unparseable trigger body and expect emitter.dacpac.statementUnrendered refusal.

findings.topic: Eject terminal package vs the P-7 contract (claim pkt-eject-bom) — REFINED: self-verification confirmed; the promised bundle/refactorlog/manifest contents are not yet in the package

v1.behavior: No eject concept exists; v1 has no timeline, episode store, or terminal-package verb.
v1.cite: src/Osm.Cli/Commands/ (no such verb; FullExportVerb.cs is the terminal verb set)

v2.behavior: projection eject --store loads the durable timeline and assembles EjectPackage = Timeline + every Episode (append-forever) + RefactorLogRefs (the REFERENCE chain, not the rendered document) + Genesis/Frozen/Reconstructed schemas; isFaithful replays genesis→latest and the CLI exits 5 on mismatch, 0 on verified. NOT in the package as coded: the rendered accumulated .refactorlog (named G3 residual 3 — 'the eject verb's slice of P-7'), the frozen SSDT bundle, the terminal ChangeManifest, the operator provenance declaration.

findings.difference: The claim's exit-5 self-verification and append-forever shape are confirmed in code; the fuller P-7 bill of materials remains contract-not-code. Since G3 landed, the bundle side of the terminal package is producible, but the eject verb does not yet assemble it.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — eject is the terminal-day operation; must close before the actual eject, not before cutover
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: build a 3-episode store (two renames), run projection eject --store; confirm exit 0 and that no rendered .refactorlog/bundle files are produced by the verb; corrupt an edge and confirm exit 5.

findings.topic: Model identity across bundles: v2 catalog.snapshot.json faithful codec vs v1 manifest Emission hash (claim pkt-scope-fidelity-artifacts) — CONFIRMED

v1.behavior: Bundle-level model identity is the manifest's Emission {Algorithm:'SHA256', Hash} — a fingerprint over model+decisions+options; opaque (equal/not-equal only), no readable model payload in the bundle.

v2.behavior: catalog.snapshot.json — the lossless CatalogCodec serialization — is written unconditionally on every bundle emission; Source.ofFile auto-detects it via the top-level codecVersion marker, enabling `diff A/ B/` to render the precise model change between two publish dirs; the live-path Run.inputDigest hashes the same canonical form into the run ledger.

findings.difference: V2 trades the opaque hash for a round-trippable snapshot + diff seam (strictly more capable: answers WHAT changed, not just WHETHER); the manifest itself no longer carries a content hash.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — 'what changed since the last release bundle' is a per-sprint receiving-team question
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: publish A and B (one column added); run the diff verb over the two dirs and confirm the added column is named; round-trip catalog.snapshot.json through Source.ofFile and re-emit byte-identically.

findings.topic: Divergence vocabulary in-band (claim pkt-manifest-tolerance-inband) — CONFIRMED: manifest carries the closed vocabulary; fidelity carries the per-run fired residual

v1.behavior: No tolerance/fidelity machinery; the nearest analogs are the free-text Unsupported messages in the manifest and the needs-remediation.sql script.

v2.behavior: manifest.unsupported = sorted ToleratedDivergence.allKnown names; fidelity.json/.txt report DATA VIOLATIONS + ACCEPTED DIVERGENCES (tolerances fired this run) + UNIQUENESS CANDIDATES; the per-run residual is Tolerance.matchedResidual = configured ∩ observed (a configured-but-unfired tolerance is not claimed; a fired-but-unconfigured one blocks the canary); a data-violation rollup also lands as one Warn envelope pointing at manifest.remediation.sql + fidelity.json.

findings.difference: V2-only honesty surface with no v1 analog; the receiving team gets a per-run, named account of every accepted divergence instead of silence.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — fidelity.txt is the runbook's step-2 gate ('read fidelity.txt')
findings.empiricalCheck: Live lane vs localhost:11433: publish over a source with NULLs in a mandatory column; confirm fidelity.txt DATA VIOLATIONS names it and manifest.remediation.sql carries the SELECT; a clean fixture yields the honest-empty report with strict residual.

findings.topic: Bundle byte-determinism across reruns: v1 embeds wall-clock; v2 pins T1

v1.behavior: PostDeployment-Bootstrap.sql embeds DateTime.UtcNow in its header on every run; pipeline-telemetry.zip carries entry timestamps; full-export.manifest.json embeds run timestamps — same model, different bytes per run.

v2.behavior: Every text artifact is byte-deterministic (T1): pinned JSON writer options, forward-slash paths, sorted orders; the staging-dir GUID never lands in artifacts; the refactorlog's ChangeDateTime is the boundary-supplied episode At (an input, not a clock read); the only non-byte-stable artifact is the opt-in dacpac (content-equality amendment, named).

findings.difference: V2 bundles diff clean across re-runs of the same model (the drift-detection seam depends on it); v1 bundles always show noise diffs.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: yes — release-to-release bundle diffs are the operator's cheapest verification tool
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: run each exporter twice into fresh dirs and `diff -r` the outputs; v2 (sans dacpac) is byte-identical, v1 differs on the template header and zip.

findings.topic: V1-only layout capabilities absent in v2: Modules.Bare dual-tree and module-name sanitization; v2 adds emissionFolders redirects

v1.behavior: EmitTableMode Both writes a parallel Modules.Bare/ tree of bare table variants (default FullOnly); SanitizeModuleNames (default true) maps non-alphanumeric module chars to '_' for folder names, with collision remapping in the seed step.

v2.behavior: No bare-table mode; module folder names are the raw Name VO (validation: non-blank only — no path-character sanitization); instead v2 adds the operator emissionFolders knob redirecting a kind's Modules/<Module>/ directory to a named folder (validated: non-empty, not absolute, no backslashes).

findings.difference: Bare-mode loss is negligible (non-default v1 mode); sanitization loss is theoretical because Service Studio constrains module names to alphanumeric+underscore — but a hostile/legacy module name would fault v2's file write where v1 sanitized. emissionFolders is a v2-only flexibility gain.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — real OutSystems module names satisfy v2's implicit assumption; external/renamed modules could not
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: feed a catalog whose module Name contains '/' or ':' through v2 publish and observe the write fault vs v1's sanitized folder; run v2 with an emissionFolders override and confirm the redirected path in manifest.json tableFile.

findings.topic: Doc-vs-code contradiction: DECISIONS.md claims 'V1 emits a refactorlog artifact' — V1 code has none

v1.behavior: No refactorlog emission exists anywhere in the v1 trunk (src/, tests/, tools/, docs/ grep empty; the sqlproj emitter writes no RefactorLog item).

v2.behavior: DECISIONS.md:4475 states 'V1 emits a `refactorlog` artifact (per the SSDT pattern) tracking…' — the mined claim pkt-g3 repeated it. The packet's G3 row itself never re-asserts the v1 claim, but the DECISIONS line stands uncorrected.

findings.difference: The premise that v2's G3 gap was a v1-parity regression is false: the refactorlog is a v2-original capability. Parity ledgers weighting G3 as 'V1 had it' overstate v1; the correct frame is v2-invents-then-wires.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: no — documentation hygiene; affects how the register weighs the gap history
findings.empiricalCheck: Static: grep -ri refactorlog over the v1 trunk (src/, tests/, tools/) — zero hits; run v1 full-export on any fixture and list output for *.refactorlog (absent).

findings.topic: Stale in-code docstring: per-lane data files '≥2 lanes' gate retired but still documented in DataEmissionComposer

v1.behavior: n/a (v1 has no per-lane data bundle; Seeds/ + Bootstrap/ + DynamicData/ trees instead).

v2.behavior: RenderedDataBundle.perLaneFiles docstring says the pipeline 'emits these only when ≥2 are present', but the wired path emits every non-empty lane (the ≥2 gate was retired with the fused-seed removal per DECISIONS 2026-06-14; the code comment at the Pipeline site says so explicitly).

findings.difference: Doc-only drift inside v2; behavior is the newer (every-non-empty-lane) contract.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: no — internal doc hygiene
findings.empiricalCheck: Fixture lane: publish with only StaticSeeds populated (single lane) and confirm Data/StaticSeeds.sql is still written.

findings.topic: Ancillary emitters (claim pkt-g7-ancillary) — CONFIRMED: migration lens never feeds the dacpac; DockerImageEmitter dormant; PhysicalSchemaReader backstop

v1.behavior: No analogs: no ALTER-preview emitter, no docker-image packaging, no in-process schema read-back (v1's check is ScriptDom parse validation of emitted text in BuildSsdtSqlValidationStep).

v2.behavior: DacpacEmitter.isSchemaStatement structurally excludes the entire imperative-migration statement family (AlterTableAddColumn/DropColumn/DropConstraint/DropIndex/DropSequence…) so SchemaMigrationEmitter output can never enter the declarative package; loss-bearing migrations gate on LossDeclaration/--allow-drops; DockerImageEmitter has zero references from Projection.Cli or Projection.Pipeline (no knob, no verb — dormant, ship-or-cut at eject); PhysicalSchemaReader is the in-process round-trip backstop.

findings.difference: All three sub-claims verified against code; the packet row is accurate on this tree.
findings.outsystemsRelevant: conditional — the migrate lens matters for in-place evolution rehearsal; none are on the bundle-deploy path
findings.empiricalCheck: Static: grep DockerImageEmitter callers (none); fixture lane: run migrate preview with a dropped column and confirm the refusal without --allow-drops.

findings.empiricalFact: Authored OutSystems empty-string default (ossys_Entity_Attr.DEFAULT_VALUE = two apostrophes) renders in V2 as DEFAULT N'''''' (a literal 2-character string of apostrophes; v2-live Modules/AppCore/dbo.Customer.sql:6,8) while V1 renders DEFAULT ('') (v1-live same file), matching the deployed constraint.
findings.mechanism: V2: MetadataSnapshotRunner reads Default_Value verbatim (ordinal 8, 'carried so the rowset path emits authored defaults') -> OssysRowsetReader lifts it with SqlLiteral.ofRaw p (Some raw), typing the authored EXPRESSION text as a literal VALUE of the column's resolved primitive; for Text that is TextLit "''" (SqlLiteral.fs:145); the DDL render (ScriptDomBuild DEFAULT clause -> buildSqlLiteral -> national StringLiteral whose Value is the 2-char string) then quote-doubles it to N''''''. The reflected #ColumnReality.DefaultDefinition (which would be ('')) is extracted but deliberately un-lifted per 'matrix row 53's named trigger' (comment at OssysRowsetReader.fs:96-103). V1: SmoEntityEmitter.ResolveDefaultExpression PREFERS attribute.OnDisk.DefaultDefinition — the deployed sys.default_constraints.definition text ('('')'), captured by extraction (outsystems_model_export.sql:368-369) — over the authored value; SmoNormalization.NormalizeSqlExpression trims redundant outer parens but its quote-guard (inner.IndexOf('\'') >= 0 -> break) preserves ('') intact; the text is then ScriptDom-parsed and re-printed (CreateTableStatementBuilder.cs:321, ParseExpression :391-409). ANSWER to 'which side interprets the OutSystems expression language': NEITHER has an interpreter. V1 sidesteps it entirely by re-emitting deployed SQL; V2 pseudo-interprets by projecting the expression text through the typed-value channel — that projection IS the misrendering. The only OutSystems-expression vocabulary either side truly interprets is boolean True/False (V1 NormalizeBitDefaultExpression SmoColumnBuilder.cs:114-123; V2 RawValueCodec.parseBoolean via BooleanLit).
findings.fixCost: Small: interpret the two-apostrophe OutSystems empty-string expression as Some "" in the OssysRowsetReader lift (TextLit "" already renders N''), or lift the already-extracted #ColumnReality.DefaultDefinition. WP coverage: partial — F11/WP-3 (LANDED) claims 'the constraint plane was always faithful (DEFAULT N'')', which this live authored-channel evidence falsifies; packet C9 ('DEFAULT literals: authored channel only') is an OPEN register row with no WP; no WP interprets authored expression defaults.
findings.notes: The golden corpus is catalog-direct so it never exercises the authored Default_Value channel — that is why WP-3's constraint-plane claim held in goldens but fails on the live estate. SqlLiteral.ofRaw's doc contract says raws come from RawValueCodec canonical forms; the authored-default lift is the one caller that violates it.

findings.empiricalFact: Authored default getutcdate() renders in V2 as DEFAULT CAST ('getutcdate()' AS DATETIME2 (7)) (v2-live Modules/Ops/dbo.JobRun.sql:9) — a string literal, proven broken at insert with Msg 241; V1 emits DEFAULT getutcdate() (works).
findings.mechanism: V1 'decides' function-vs-literal by never deciding: ResolveDefaultExpression returns the deployed definition '(getutcdate())' (extracted at outsystems_model_export.sql:369); TrimRedundantParentheses strips the outer parens because the inner text contains no quote (SmoNormalization.cs:28-49) -> 'getutcdate()'; ScriptDom's ParseExpression (TSql150Parser) parses it as a FunctionCall and the generator re-prints it bare (CreateTableStatementBuilder.cs:391-409). V2 fails to decide at the same lift as item 1: OssysRowsetReader.fs:104-108 calls SqlLiteral.ofRaw with primitive DateTime, so 'getutcdate()' becomes DateTimeLit "getutcdate()" (SqlLiteral.fs:157-158 — no validation that the raw is a canonical temporal form), and the WP-17(d) explicit-CAST temporal render wraps any DateTimeLit raw in CAST('<raw>' AS datetime2(7)) (SqlLiteral.fs:176-179 for text plane; ScriptDomBuild.fs:338-345 temporalCast for the ScriptDom plane, reached from the DEFAULT clause via buildSqlLiteral at ScriptDomBuild.fs:503). WP-17(d)'s data-plane CAST form thus leaks onto the DEFAULT DDL surface through the shared literal builder.
findings.fixCost: Small-to-medium: the lift needs an expression-vs-value discriminator (e.g. refuse or pass through raws that fail RawValueCodec temporal parse, or consume the reflected DefaultDefinition). WP coverage: NONE for authored expression defaults (C9 register open, unnumbered); WP-17(d) is LANDED and is the proximate source of the CAST shape; WP-9 covers only default-constraint NAMES.
findings.notes: Same producing pipeline as finding 1; one fix closes both. V1's correctness here is inherited from database reality, not from any function/literal classifier — there is no such classifier anywhere in V1.

findings.empiricalFact: V1 emits no ON DELETE/ON UPDATE clause for any FK (zero occurrences in v1-live/out; deployed catalog all NO_ACTION) even though the model says Delete/SetNull and ParityEstate has CASCADE, SET_NULL and ON UPDATE CASCADE (sqlcmd-verified); V2 preserves all of them.
findings.mechanism: The data survives extraction and ingestion: outsystems_model_export.sql:586-587 selects fk.delete_referential_action_desc/update_referential_action_desc, :688-689 emits them as JSON onDelete/onUpdate ('CASCADE', 'SET_NULL', 'NO_ACTION'); Osm.Json maps them into RelationshipActualConstraint.OnDeleteAction (RelationshipDocumentMapper.cs:109-115). The drop is in Osm.Smo: ForeignKeyEvidenceResolver prefers constraint.OnDeleteAction over the model rule (ForeignKeyEvidenceResolver.cs:141-143) and feeds it to SmoEntityEmitter.MapDeleteRule (:170-186) — a CASE-SENSITIVE ordinal switch over the OutSystems model vocabulary {"Cascade","Delete","Protect","Ignore","SetNull"}; the sys-catalog vocabulary ('CASCADE'/'SET_NULL'/'NO_ACTION') matches nothing and falls to `_ => ForeignKeyAction.NoAction`. Emission then omits the clause for NoAction (inline path CreateTableStatementBuilder.cs:175-178; NOCHECK path :265-271). ON UPDATE is structurally unrepresentable: SmoForeignKeyDefinition has no UpdateAction field at all (SmoModel.cs:111-120). NOT config-gated: config/default-tightening.json foreignKeys toggles (enableCreation/allowCrossSchema/allowNoCheckCreation) gate creation and NOCHECK scripting only; nothing configurable reaches MapDeleteRule, so NO V1 config could have preserved delete rules on physically-backed FKs. Perversely, only a logical-only reference (no deployed constraint) would get a real action, via the fallback's model-code path (ForeignKeyFallbackFactory.cs:64, 'Delete'->Cascade).
findings.cite: v1: src/Osm.Smo/SmoEntityEmitter.cs:170-186 (the vocabulary/case mismatch) + src/Osm.Smo/ForeignKeyEvidenceResolver.cs:141-143 + src/Osm.Smo/SmoModel.cs:111-120 (no UpdateAction) + src/Osm.Smo/PerTableEmission/CreateTableStatementBuilder.cs:175-178,265-271,544-549. v2 contrast: sidecar/projection/src/Projection.Adapters.Osm/OssysTranslation.fs:458-461 (chooseOnDeleteAction, WP-1b) + OssysRowsetReader.fs:220-232 (parseSqlForeignKeyAction — V2 found and fixed the SAME vocabulary bug 2026-05-19) + sidecar/projection/src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/ScriptDomBuild.fs:581-604 (renders DeleteAction and UpdateAction)
findings.fixCost: Trivial for ON DELETE (make MapDeleteRule accept both vocabularies); small for ON UPDATE (add the field + render). WP coverage: E1/WP-1b LANDED is the V2-side fix (reflected #FkReality.DeleteAction wins + divergence diagnostic); there is no V1-side WP — V1 is the frozen donor.
findings.notes: V2's comment at OssysRowsetReader.fs:225-231 documents the identical bug class ('routed through parseDeleteRule which silently dropped every valid SQL Server value') caught by FkRealityRowsetRoundTripTests — V1 never got that test. Packet §1.1 row 2 / E1 describes the delete-rule reality problem as V2's headline; this finding shows V1's behavior is strictly worse (everything NO_ACTION).

findings.empiricalFact: V1 emitted Category.ParentCategoryId and StockMovement.SupplierId as NOT NULL although the model says nullable (policy-decision-report.json: MakeNotNull=true, rationales DATA_NO_NULLS + FK_ENFORCED); V2 emitted both NULL, matching model and deployed estate.
findings.mechanism: V1 evidence-gated tightening: the live run's config (cfg.v1.live.json -> config/default-tightening.json) sets policy.mode=EvidenceGated, nullBudget=0.0. TighteningPolicyMatrix's EvidenceGated row makes the ForeignKey signal a Tighten participant with RequiresEvidence:true (TighteningPolicyMatrix.cs:129-159); NullabilitySignalFactory wraps ForeignKeySupportSignal in RequiresEvidenceSignal paired with NullEvidenceSignal (NullabilitySignalFactory.cs:41-44). NullEvidenceSignal fires DATA_NO_NULLS when the profiled NullCount is 0 (or within the 0.0 budget — IsWithinNullBudget, NullEvidenceSignal.cs:39-66); ForeignKeySupportSignal supplies FK_ENFORCED for an enforced/creatable FK. Both columns profiled 0 nulls with a deployed FK -> MakeNotNull. The decision is consumed at emission: SmoEntityEmitter.ShouldEnforceNotNull returns decision.MakeNotNull (SmoEntityEmitter.cs:85-96) and SmoColumnBuilder sets nullable = !ShouldEnforceNotNull (SmoColumnBuilder.cs:26). V2 has NO such coercion on this run: column nullability is pure source truth — IsNullable = not row.IsMandatory (OssysRowsetReader.fs:79); V2's NullabilityPass exists but is decision-only with 'observable identity on empty policy — V2 takes no action on tightening unless explicitly directed' (NullabilityPass.fs:31-33), and the live projection.json declares no tightening interventions.
findings.fixCost: Config-level on V1 (mode=Cautious or policy.nullabilityOverrides suppress it; it is deliberate EvidenceGated behavior, not a bug). WP coverage: packet C6 and §1.1 row 4 record this as a by-design V1 divergence from database reality; no WP — V2's posture (no coercion without an explicit intervention) is the plan-of-record.
findings.notes: 'At fault' here means V1 is the side producing the divergence from source; it is intended behavior under its shipped defaults. Risk realized downstream: the NOT NULL SupplierId collides with the FK's authored SET NULL delete rule (which V1 also dropped — finding 3 masks a deploy-time contradiction V1 would otherwise have).

findings.empiricalFact: V1's JobRun.sql:14-16 puts CREATE TRIGGER [dbo].[TR_JobRun_AUDIT] and ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE TRIGGER [TR_OSUSR_XYZ_JOBRUN_AUDIT] in ONE batch (no GO) — the ALTER becomes part of the trigger module (sys.sql_modules) and the trigger deploys ENABLED (catalog-v1 is_disabled=0), firing Msg 4920 on insert; the DISABLE also names the stale physical trigger. V2 batches correctly but keeps the physical trigger name AND physical ON target ([dbo].[OSUSR_XYZ_JOBRUN]) — CREATE TRIGGER fails deploy with Msg 8197 (v2-deploy: FAIL dbo.JobRun.sql, dbo.StockMovement.sql).
findings.mechanism: V1: PerTableWriter.BuildTriggerScripts concatenates comment + rewritten CREATE TRIGGER + (if IsDisabled) the ALTER ... DISABLE into ONE StringBuilder and adds it as ONE list element (PerTableWriter.cs:301-322); StatementBatchFormatter.JoinStatements inserts GO only BETWEEN list elements (StatementBatchFormatter.cs:44-55), so no separator lands inside — a CREATE TRIGGER batch extends to the batch end, absorbing the ALTER into the module body. The name mismatch: the DISABLE uses trigger.Name — the raw physical name (PerTableWriter.cs:317) — while the CREATE's name was renamed only as an incidental side effect of RewriteTriggerDefinition's raw case-insensitive substring replace of the physical table name inside the whole definition (OSUSR_XYBRUN...: 'OSUSR_XYZ_JOBRUN'->'JobRun' also rewrites the embedded trigger name TR_OSUSR_XYZ_JOBRUN_AUDIT -> TR_JobRun_AUDIT; PerTableWriter.cs:327-354, table-name replaces :340-342, which also retarget the ON clause). V2: SsdtDdlEmitter.triggerStatements emits Comment / Statement.CreateTrigger t.Definition / AlterTableDisableTrigger as SEPARATE typed statements (SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:575-589 — correct GO batching, and the DISABLE names the same physical trigger the CREATE declares, internally consistent); but t.Definition is the RAW deployed body from sys.sql_modules (MetadataSnapshotRunner.fs:333-343,664-669) and tryParseTriggerBody parses/re-prints it with NO logical rewrite (ScriptDomBuild.fs:2570-2577) — so the ON clause still targets the physical table that does not exist in the projected DB -> Msg 8197.
findings.cite: v1: src/Osm.Smo/PerTableWriter.cs:301-322 (one-element script, DISABLE stale name :317), :327-354 (rename via raw replace), src/Osm.Smo/PerTableEmission/StatementBatchFormatter.cs:44-55 (GO only between elements). v2: sidecar/projection/src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:575-589; sidecar/projection/src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/ScriptDomBuild.fs:2570-2577; sidecar/projection/src/Projection.Adapters.OssysSql/MetadataSnapshotRunner.fs:333-343
findings.fixCost: V1: trivial (emit the ALTER as its own list element; use the rewritten trigger name) — no WP, V1 defect. V2: WP-6 (H2) is the planned fix — ScriptDom-based literal-safe identifier rewriting extended to trigger bodies ('done means: the golden trigger targets [dbo].[ScalarGallery]'); also aligns text path to refusal for unparseable bodies.
findings.notes: Two independent V1 defects compound: without the missing GO the stale name would 'only' fail at deploy; with it, deploy SUCCEEDS silently (the ALTER parses as module text) and the failure moves to first-insert runtime (Msg 4920 from inside the enabled trigger) — the worst of the three outcomes. V2's failure is loud at deploy (the better failure mode), and its packet row H2 documents 'trigger bodies ship with physical table references' as a known gap.

findings.empiricalFact: V2's Data/Bootstrap.sql orders the Customer MERGE (CityId=1) before the City MERGE (lines 1-13) — FK 547 proven on linear execution (roundtrip: FAIL Data/Bootstrap.sql).
findings.mechanism: Topological ordering IS applied to the bootstrap lane render — renderArtifactInTopoOrder walks topo.Order for all three data lanes (DataEmissionComposer.fs:336-348, Bootstrap at :500-502) — but the order itself is degraded. Chain sequence: SymmetricClosure runs BEFORE TopologicalOrderPass (RegisteredTransforms.fs:124 vs :128). SymmetricClosure attaches a derived INVERSE navigation reference on every FK target kind (SymmetricClosure.fs:5-12, buildInverse — catalog.snapshot.json shows City carrying 'D7:inverse...' -> Customer). TopologicalOrderPass.buildGraphWithin folds over ALL Kind.References with no Reference.isInverse filter (TopologicalOrderPass.fs:155-201), so every FK plus its inverse forms a 2-cycle. CycleResolution.classify (Strategies/CycleResolution.fs:58-67) marks an edge Weak only if the source attribute is nullable with NoAction/SetNull: the real FK Customer.CityId is mandatory -> Other; the inverse's source attribute is the target's PK -> Other. Zero-Weak SCC -> weakFeedbackStrategy refuses (run.ndjson: 'unresolvable: a cycle of non-deferrable FK edges among [bbbbbbbb...0001, bbbbbbbb...0002]'), and ONE unresolved SCC degrades the ENTIRE catalog order: Mode=Alphabetical, Order = graph.Nodes (TopologicalOrderPass.fs:541-556) — and 'alphabetical' is actually SsKey-sorted node order (:137-138). Customer's SsKey (...0001) < City's (...0002), so Customer renders first. Contrast inside V2 itself: the DDL emitter EXCLUDES inverses via Reference.isDeployable (SsdtDdlEmitter.fs:358-366, 'inverses are navigation edges; they never reach a constraint-emission surface') — which is why the FK DDL is correct while the ordering graph is poisoned. Internal deploy leg: runFullExportLoad -> Deploy.executeLeveledSeed (Faces/Export.fs:166) -> composeRenderedLeveled* -> TopologicalOrder.levels, which under Alphabetical mode returns SINGLETON groups in the SAME degraded t.Order (TopologicalOrder.fs:313-317: 'the leveled deploy equals the sequential one exactly') — NOT differently ordered; the --load leg would hit the same 547.
findings.fixCost: Small and surgical: filter Reference.isInverse out of buildGraphWithin (inverses are navigation-only by the emitter's own rule) — that alone makes this estate acyclic and restores City-first. WP coverage: WP-13 (E8, 'cycle-fallback ordering') is the plan-of-record for the fallback behavior, but the packet frames E8 as genuine-schema-cycle fallback; the inverse-edge poisoning that manufactures cycles out of a DAG is not named in any WP.
findings.notes: The run log proves a 5-member and a 3-member 'unresolved cycle' on an estate whose real FK graph is nearly a tree — direct evidence the cycles are synthetic (inverse edges). One genuine weak 2-cycle (0004<->0005) auto-resolved correctly. The SsKey fallback order looks alphabetical in the diagnostics vocabulary but is GUID-ordered, which is why the FK child (Customer, created first in ossys) precedes its parent.

findings.empiricalFact: V1's bundle omits platform-auto index OSIDX_JOBRUN_CREATEDON (absent from v1-live/out and catalog-v1); V2 emits it as IX_JobRun_CreatedOn (v2-live Modules/Ops/dbo.JobRun.sql; catalog.diff line 82).
findings.mechanism: V1: extraction flags isPlatformAuto by the name-prefix heuristic OSIDX\_% (outsystems_model_export.sql:836); the emission default is PRUNE — TighteningOptions.Default sets includePlatformAutoIndexes:false (TighteningOptions.cs:60-66, value at :62), the shipped config repeats it (config/default-tightening.json:32), SmoBuildOptions.Default mirrors it (SmoBuildOptions.cs:21); the filter executes at SmoIndexBuilder.cs:74 — skip when (!IncludePlatformAutoIndexes && index.IsPlatformAuto && !index.IsUnique), i.e. prune keeps unique auto-indexes. V2: the same-named knob defaults to KEEP — config binder default IncludePlatformAutoIndexes=true (Config.fs:531, read at :1343) and EmissionPolicy default true (Policy.fs:758); the pipeline-wired filter EmissionPolicy.filterPlatformAutoIndexes is identity under true (Policy.fs:831-844; seam wiring EmissionSeam.fs:42-47). Compounding: V2's LIVE rowset path never populates Index.IsPlatformAuto at all — 'rowset path doesn't surface it; it lives on V1's logical IndexModel projection' (OssysRowsetReader.fs:458-460; IR default false, Catalog.fs:1488,1513) — so on a live extraction even includePlatformAutoIndexes:false could not drop OSIDX (only the V1-JSON adapter maps the flag, OssysJsonReader.fs:408-427). The IX_JobRun_CreatedOn name is V2's synthesized logical index name (IndexNaming.fs:74-75).
findings.fixCost: Config flip is NOT sufficient on the live path — the rowset reader must also derive IsPlatformAuto (trivial: same OSIDX_ prefix heuristic). WP coverage: no WP; packet D2 register [KNOB, verdict open] and §1.1 row 8 document both divergences explicitly (V1 shipped default prune-keeping-unique vs V2 default keep-all, and the irreversibility after renaming).
findings.notes: 'sideAtFault: v2' is relative to V1-parity: which side to bless is an open D2 verdict — keep inherits redundant auto-indexes, prune loses FK-column indexes (D6: nothing synthesizes replacements).
